### p3520[0...7] CI: Infeed power precontrol signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7603 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources for power precontrol.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Power 0  
[
1]:
Power 1  
[
2]:
Power 2  
[
3]:
Power 3  
[
4]:
Power 4  
[
5]:
Power 5  
[
6]:
Power 6  
[
7]:
Power 7

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3521

**Note:**
  
Closed-loop control of the DC link voltage is improved by precontrolling the power
required for the other modules. A non-scaled quantity is expected so that the various
power reference values (r2004) of the drive objects do not have to be taken into account.
The scaling factors are used to adapt the scaling (p3521).

4.7

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Power 0  
[
1]:
Power 1  
[
2]:
Power 2  
[
3]:
Power 3  
[
4]:
Power 4  
[
5]:
Power 5  
[
6]:
Power 6  
[
7]:
Power 7  
[
8]:
Total

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3520, p3521

**Note:**
  
These values are used to set the correct power scaling for the relevant precontrol.  
[0]: p3520[0] * p3521[0]  
[1]: p3520[1] * p3521[1]  
[2]: p3520[2] * p3521[2]  
[3]: p3520[3] * p3521[3]  
[4]: p3520[4] * p3521[4]  
[5]: p3520[5] * p3521[5]  
[6]: p3520[6] * p3521[6]  
[7]: p3520[7] * p3521[7]  
[8]: sum [0] ... [7]

### p3610 Infeed reactive current fixed setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**6_2 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -10000.0 [Arms] | 10000.0 [Arms] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [Arms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Sets the fixed setpoint for the reactive current.  
p3610 &lt; 0:  
Inductive reactive current is produced, i. e. the current follows the voltage. The
converter behaves just like an inductance connected to the line supply.  
p3610 &gt; 0:  
Inductive capacitive current is produced, i. e. the voltage follows the current. The
converter behaves just like a capacitor connected to the line supply.

Sets the signal source for the supplementary setpoint of the reactive current.  
p3610 &lt; 0:  
Inductive reactive current is produced, i. e. the current follows the voltage. The
converter behaves just like an inductance connected to the line supply.  
p3610 &gt; 0:  
Inductive capacitive current is produced, i. e. the voltage follows the current. The
converter behaves just like a capacitor connected to the line supply.

transformer. Generally, this does not mean that it has to have a secondary voltage
of 100 V (phase-phase, rms).

### p3890[0...n] Short-circuit current limit

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

4.4

### p3891[0...n] Short circuit voltage threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [Vrms] | 100000 [Vrms] | [ 0 ] 0 [Vrms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the voltage threshold for the short-circuit detection.  
A short circuit is detected if, during operation, the output voltage falls below the
value specified in p3891 and the absolute current value reaches the short-circuit
limit (p3890). In this case, alarm A06849 is first output, followed later by fault
F06850, and then the clear time starts (p3892). The converter shuts down if the absolute
current value is still at the short-circuit limit after the clear time has expired.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3890, p3892  
Refer to:
F06850

### p3895[0...n] Current limiting controller response threshold dynamic

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

here, then the short-circuit current limiting intervenes.  
The percentage value refers to the rated converter current (r0207).

### p3900 Completion of quick commissioning

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Exits quick commissioning (p0010 = 1) with automatic calculation of all parameters
of all existing drive data sets that depend on the entries made during quick commissioning.  
p3900 = 1 initially includes a parameter reset (factory setting, the same as p0970
= 1) for all parameters of the drive object; however, without overwriting the entries
made during the quick commissioning.  
The interconnections of PROFIBUS PZD telegram selection (p0922) and the interconnections
via p0700, p1000 and p1500 are re-established and all of the dependent motor, open-loop
and control-loop control parameters are calculated (corresponding to p0340 = 1).  
p3900 = 2 includes the restoration of the interconnections of PROFIBUS PZD telegram
selection (p0922) and the interconnections via p0700, p1000 and p1500 and the calculations
corresponding to p0340 = 1.  
p3900 = 3 only includes the calculations associated with the motor, open-loop and
closed-loop control parameters corresponding to p0340 = 1.

**Value:**
  
0:
No quick parameterization  
1:
Quick parameterization after parameter reset  
2:
Quick parameterization (only) for BICO and motor parameters  
3:
Quick parameterization for motor parameters (only)

**Notice:**
  
After the value has been modified, no further parameter modifications can be made
and the status is shown in r3996. Modifications can be made again when r3996 = 0.

**Note:**
  
When the calculations have been completed, p3900 and p0010 are automatically reset
to a value of zero.  
When calculating motor, open-loop and closed-loop control parameters (such as for
p0340 = 1) parameters associated with a selected Siemens catalog motor are not overwritten.

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the counter reading for modified BICO interconnections on this drive object.  
The counter is incremented by one for each modified BICO interconnection.

### p3981 Acknowledge drive object faults

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8060 |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Setting to acknowledge all active faults of a drive object.

**Notice:**
  
Safety messages cannot be acknowledged using this parameter.

**Note:**
  
Parameter should be set from 0 to 1 to acknowledge.  
After acknowledgment, the parameter is automatically reset to 0.

### p3985 Master control mode selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Setpoints | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode to change over the master control / LOCAL mode.

**Value:**
  
0:
Change master control for STW1.0 = 0  
1:
Change master control in operation

**Danger:**
  
When changing the master control in operation, the drive can manifest undesirable
behavior - e.g. it can accelerate up to another setpoint.

### r3986 Number of parameters

|  |  |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |

Displays the number of parameters for this drive unit.

### p3990 Sequentially resets drive parameters

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**All groups | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 300 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Parameter to internally calculate subsequent (follow-on) parameters after a factory
setting was carried out.  
The parameter is then reset back to 0.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Start a parameter reset  
3:
Start download of volatile parameters from RAM  
5:
Starts a safety parameter reset  
6:
Start reset non-safety/safety parameters  
10:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 10  
11:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 11  
12:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 12  
30:
Start loading delivery condition saved with p0971 = 30  
100:
Start a BICO interconnection reset  
300:
Only Siemens internal

**Dependency:**
  
This parameter is called when first commissioning the system and also for factory
settings if this has been initiated via p0970 or p3900.  
  
Refer to:
p3900

### r3996[0...1] Parameter write inhibit status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Displays whether writing to parameters is inhibited.  
r3996[0] = 0:  
Parameter write not inhibited.  
0 &lt; r3996[0] &lt; 100:  
Parameter write inhibited. The value shows how the calculations are progressing.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Progress calculations  
[
1]:
Cause

**Note:**
  
For index [1]:  
Only for internal Siemens troubleshooting.

### r4021[0...n] CO: PSA4 digital inputs terminal actual value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9982, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Displays the actual value of the digital inputs/outputs of the Power Stack Adapter
(PSA4).  
This means that the actual input signal can be checked at terminal DI x prior to switching
from the simulation mode (p4095.x = 1) to the terminal mode (p4095.x = 0).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | DI 0 (X322.8) gate supply voltage (GSV) faulted | Yes | No | - |
| 01 | DI 1 (X322.5) cabinet door opened | Yes | No | - |
| 02 | DI 2 (X322.4) | Low | High | - |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | Low | High | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) (status fan 1 on) | Low | High | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) (status fan 2 on) | Low | High | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) | Low | High | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) | Low | High | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) (protective breaker GSV tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) (protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) (protective breaker PSA tripped) | Low | High | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Low | High | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Low | High | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Low | High | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Low | High | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) (prot. breaker anticondens. heating tripped) | Low | High | - |

**Note:**
  
DI: Digital Input  
The significance of the inputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | Low | High | - |

### r4047[0...n] PSA4 digital outputs status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9983, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Displays the status of the digital outputs of Power Stack Adapter (PSA4).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 08 | DO 0 (X331.15) command circuit breaker undervoltage coil | Yes | No | - |
| 09 | DO 1 (X331.14) enable circuit breaker | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | DO 2 (X331.11) reserved | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | DO 3 (X331.10) command door release on | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | DO 4 (X331.7) command fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | DO 5 (X331.6) command fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | DO 6 (X331.3) command fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | Low | High | - |
| 15 | DO 7 (X331.2) command fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | Low | High | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) protective breaker GSV tripped | Low | High | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped | Low | High | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) protective breaker PSA tripped | Low | High | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) status fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Low | High | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) status fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Low | High | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) status fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | Low | High | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) status fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | Low | High | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) protective breaker anticondens. heating tripped | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p4028, p4048

**Note:**
  
Inversion using p4048 has been taken into account.  
DO: Digital Output  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output  
The significance of the outputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The outputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

### p4048[0...n] PSA4 invert digital outputs

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9983, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Setting to invert the signals at the digital outputs of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA4).  
The inversion is only effective for the freely interconnectable digital outputs.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 10 | DO 2 (X331.11) reserved | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Invert DO 3 (X331.10) command door release on | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Invert DO 4 (X331.7) command fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 13 | Invert DO 5 (X331.6) command fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 14 | Invert DO 6 (X331.3) command fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 15 | Invert DO 7 (X331.2) command fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 16 | Invert DI/DO 0 (X321.13) protective breaker GSV tripped | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 17 | Invert DI/DO 1 (X321.12) protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 18 | Invert DI/DO 2 (X321.11) protective breaker PSA tripped | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 19 | Invert DI/DO 3 (X321.10) status fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 20 | Invert DI/DO 4 (X321.6) status fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 21 | Invert DI/DO 5 (X321.5) status fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 22 | Invert DI/DO 6 (X321.4) status fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 23 | Invert DI/DO 7 (X321.3) prot. breaker anti-cond. heating tripped | Not inverted | Inverted | - |

**Note:**
  
DO: Digital Output  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output  
The inversion is only effective for the freely interconnectable digital outputs.  
When the signal is configured as input, then the associated bit in p4048 has no function.

| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the simulation mode for the digital inputs of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA4).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) (status fan 1 on) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) (status fan 2 on) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) (protective breaker GSV tripped) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) (protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) (protective breaker PSA tripped) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) (prot. breaker anticondens. heating tripped) | Terminal eval | Simulation | - |

**Dependency:**
  
The setpoint for the input signals is specified using p4096.  
  
Refer to:
p4096

**Note:**
  
This parameter is not saved when data is backed-up (p0971, p0977).  
DI: Digital Input  
The significance of the inputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

in r4863/r4864.

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the GUIDs of the Technology Extensions installed on this drive object.  
r4959[0...15]: GUID of Technology Extension 1  
r4959[16]: Major information of Technology Extension 1  
r4959[17]: Minor information of Technology Extension 1  
r4959[18...33]: GUID of Technology Extension 2  
r4959[34]: Major information of Technology Extension 2  
r4959[35]: Minor information of Technology Extension 2, ...

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r4950, r4951, r4952, r4955, p4956, r4957, r4958, r4960

**Notice:**
  
This parameter is only indexed if at least one drive object-specific Technology Extension
exists (p4950 &gt; 0).

**Note:**
  
TEC: Technology Extension

### p5401[0...n] BI: Line droop control activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** 7982 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source to activate line droop control for the infeed.  
BI: p5401 = 1 signal:  
Activates the line supply droop control.  
BI: p5401 = 0 signal:  
Deactivates the line supply droop control.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r5402

**Note:**
  
The line droop control can only be deactivated using p5401 if the signal "feedback
switch open" is set (r6690[0].2 = 1) and line voltage sensing is available (p0140).

### r5402.0...8 CO/BO: Line droop control status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the status word of line droop control for the infeed.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Line droop control active | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Internal synchronism ok | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 08:  
The converter output voltage follows the line voltage sensing.  
Notice:  
Possibly set phase and frequency offsets in the phase control loop of the line voltage
(p6438/p6439) are also realized in the output voltage. This means that absolute synchronism
between the measuring voltage of the VSM and the converter output voltage only exists
if p6439 = 0 and p6438 = 0.

### p5403[0...1] CI: Line droop control current signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7982 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2002 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0   [ 1 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the current to be regulated in alpha/beta coordinates.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Alpha  
[
1]:
Beta

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5404

**Note:**
  
The following BICO interconnections are recommended:  
- droop control for current/voltage at the line filter: BI: p5403 = rxxxx (BI: p5404
= ryyyy must be set)  
- droop control for current/voltage at the line transformer: BI: p5403 = rxxxx (BI:
p5404 = ryyyy must be set)

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2001 | **Expert list:**1 |

| - | - | [ 0 ] 0   [ 1 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the voltage to be regulated in alpha/beta coordinates.

**Recommend.:**
  
Set the same signal source for connector input p5404[0, 1] and p5425[0, 1].

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Alpha  
[
1]:
Beta

**Note:**
  
The following BICO interconnections are recommended:  
- droop control for current/voltage at the line filter: BI: p5404 = rxxxx (BI: p5403
= ryyyy must be set)  
- droop control for current/voltage at the line transformer: BI: p5404 = rxxxx (BI:
p5403 = ryyyy must be set)

### p5405[0...n] Line droop control frequency droop no-load frequency

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 7982 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 300.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the no-load frequency (as a % of p0211) for the line droop control active power
frequency droop.  
Droop formula (without smoothing):  
r5410 = (p5405 + p5406 + p5407 x r5411[0] / r0206) x p0211

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5409

**Note:**
  
The droop characteristic input variable is the active power r5411[0] at the selected
connection point (p5403, p5404).  
The output frequency calculated using the above formula is filtered in accordance
with the parameterized smoothing time (p5409).  
The smoothed output frequency is displayed in r5410.

### p5406[0...n] CI: Line droop control frequency droop supplementary setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** 7982 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the frequency droop supplementary setpoint (as a % of p0211).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5405

**Note:**
  
For index [0]:  
The setpoint signals are smoothed using a PT1 filter (p5409).  
For index [1]:  
If the signals for the unsmoothed setpoints are precisely reset to 0 (e.g. for p5483[3]
= 1), then by internally adapting the smoothed setpoint state, an undesirable step-like
frequency change is avoided. The signal for the smoothed setpoint should be adapted
using a corresponding frequency change, if the frequency is to be kept constant.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 100.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 5.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the gradient of the frequency droop (as a % of the rated frequency p0211 at the
rated power r0206).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5405

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**

### p5409[0...n] Line droop control frequency droop smoothing time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 7982 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [ms] | 10000.00 [ms] | [ 0 ] 150.00 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the smoothing time for the output frequency of the active power frequency droop
(r5410).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5405

**Note:**
  
When the load changes, mechanical energy generation units induce a delayed change
in frequency on account of their inertia. The converter tries to emulate this response
with the assistance of the smoothing time.  
For the line to remain stable, all the energy generation units in a separate network
have to respond in a similar manner during operation.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| - [Hz] | - [Hz] | [ ] - [Hz] |

Displays the smoothed output frequency of the active power frequency droop.

### r5411[0...1] CO: Line droop control frequency droop active power

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7982 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |

**Valid as of version:**

**Description:**
  
Displays the input active power of the active power frequency droop.  
The active power is calculated for the currents and voltages defined with connector
inputs p5403 and p5404.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Unsmoothed  
[
1]:
Smoothed

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5409

**Note:**
  
For index [1]:  
The value is smoothed using a PT1 filter (smoothing time: p0045).

### p5415[0...n] Line droop control voltage droop no-load voltage

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 7982 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 300.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the no-load voltage (as a % of p0210) for the reactive current voltage droop
for line droop control.  
Droop formula (without smoothing):  
p5420 = (p5415 + p5416 + p5417 * r5421[0] / r0207) * p0210

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5419

**Note:**
  
The droop characteristic input variable is the reactive power r5421[0] at the selected
connection point (p5403, p5404).  
The output voltage calculated using the above formula is filtered in accordance with
the parameterized smoothing time p5419.  
The smoothed output voltage is displayed in r5420.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** 7982 |

4.4

Sets the signal source for the voltage droop supplementary setpoint (as a % of p0210).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5415

For index [1]:  
Setpoint steps without smoothing can result in significant equalization operations
in the line supply and the overload of the inverter and the line components.

### p5419[0...n] Line droop control voltage droop smoothing time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 7982 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the smoothing time for the reactive current actual value (r5421[1]).  
The smoothed reactive current is the input quantity for the reactive current voltage
droop.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5415

**Note:**
  
When the load changes, mechanical energy generation units induce a delayed change
in voltage on account of their electrical properties. The converter tries to emulate
this response with the assistance of the smoothing time.  
For the line to remain stable, all the energy generation units in a separate network
have to respond in a similar manner during operation.

For index [1]:  
The value is smoothed using a PT1 filter (smoothing time: p5419).

### p5436 Direct component controller P gain

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7983 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0000 [ohm] | 10.0000 [ohm] | [ 0 ] 0.0200 [ohm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Sets the proportional gain for the PI controller to suppress the direct component
in the converter current.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p5437

**Note:**
  
Recommended setting: p5436 = total resistance of reactor and transformer.  
The parameter is preset when selecting the power unit (p201). In so doing, as approximation,
a transformer with the same rating and a total power loss of 1.5 % is assumed:  
p5436 = 1.5 % * r0206[0] / (3 * r0207[0] * r0207[0])

### p5956 Automatic restart speed threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 6762, 6763 |
| **Object:**VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 120.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 50.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the speed threshold for the automatic restart or buffering brief power failures.  
For the correspondingly parameterized (p1210) and enabled function (p1208[3]), the
following applies:  
- the function is executed above this speed threshold.  
- the function is not executed or a function that is running is interrupted below
this speed threshold.

**Note:**
  
The monitoring is deactivated with p9556 = 0 %.

### p6026[0...7] BI: PU door release enable signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9827 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0...6 ] 1   [ 7 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources for the electromagnetic door release enable.  
The signals that are read in are ANDed with the internal states such as 'U=0', 'pulse
inhibit' and 'circuit breaker open' and output at Power Stack Adapter output DO3.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Internal 1  
[
1]:
Internal 2  
[
2]:
Internal 3  
[
3]:
External 1  
[
4]:
External 2  
[
5]:
External 3  
[
6]:
Static  
[
7]:
Temporary

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6027

| 01 | Internal enable 2 | Low | High | - |
| 02 | Internal enable 3 | Low | High | - |
| 03 | External enable 1 | Low | High | - |
| 04 | External enable 2 | Low | High | - |

| 07 | Temporary enable 1 | Low | High | - |

p6026

### r6044 CO: PU I2t t_remain

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [s] | - [s] | [ ] - [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for remaining time of the I2t power unit monitoring.  
The remaining time is that time calculated by the I2t model until 100% thermal utilization
is reached (r6045). The time depends on the actual current as well as the utilization
level.  
Operation below the permissible rated current -&gt; r6044 = 3600 s  
Operation above the permissible rated current -&gt; r6044 &lt; 3600 s

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6045

**Note:**
  
Example:  
Operation with the permissible rated current (1110 A)  
-&gt; r6044 = 3600 s  
The current increases and remains approximately constant (1110 ... 1120 A)  
-&gt; r6044 = 55 s (fluctuating between 25 and 200 s), r6045 = approx. 99 %  
Current increases further  
-&gt; r6044 = 0 s, r6045 = 100 %, shutdown (F30005)

### p6060[0...1] BI: External command switch in power unit cooling signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), VECTORGL, VECTOR3P (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), VECTORM2C (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), A_INFMV (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), B_INFMV (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), A_INFM2C (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit), BMM2C (Cool_sys_ctrl, Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the external command "switch on power unit cooling".  
BI: p6060 = 0 signal  
If there is no internal switch-on signal, then the power unit cooling is switched
off.  
Exceptions:  
- when the run-on time (p0264) is appropriately set.  
- when fault F49156 occurs.  
BI: p6060 = 1 signal  
The drive converter cooling is switched on.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Redundancy subsystem 1  
[
1]:
Redundancy subsystem 2

**Note:**
  
For redundancy, the cooling units of both subsystems are switched on using their own
index

### r6071.0...6 CO/BO: PU cell bypass status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the status of the bypass switch of a double submodule
(DSM).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Bypass enabled | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Component with bypass | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Bypass activated | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Bypass deselected | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Bypass voltage monitoring active | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | All cells fault-free power unit 1 | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | All cells fault-free power unit 2 | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6070  
Refer to:
A30206

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
Bypass is activated in the power unit description data.  
For bit 01:  
Only components with bypass connection board are installed This display should only
be evaluated if a voltage is available at the cells.  
For bit 02:  
Bypass functionality is selected using parameter p6070, and is activated in the power
unit description data.  
For bit 03:  
Bypass functionality is deselected using parameter p6070, and is activated in the
power unit description data (see A30206).  
For bit 04:  
Is not used  
For bit 05:  
All double submodules are fault-free

### p6112 Bypass DSM number

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 9999 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Selection to set the bypass response of a double submodule (DSM).

**Note:**
  
The numbering of the DSM is identical to that in the alarm value of alarm A30209.

### p6113[0...n] Bypass DSM marking

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Setting to mark a double submodule as bypassed or not bypassed.  
The value for the DSM specified in p6112 is displayed.  
1 corresponds to bypassed  
0 corresponds to not bypassed

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6078, r6079, r6080, p6112

**Note:**
  
Before changing the bypass marking, the DSM number must be entered into p6112.  
If the DSM is marked as being bypassed (r6078, r6079, r6080), then by entering a change
of the value from 1 to 0, it can be marked as being not bypassed – and vice versa.

### p6215 BI: Input transformer premagnetization completed

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for feedback signal 'premagnetization completed'. The command
to switch on premagnetization is output in the control commands via r6221 bit 10.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6221

**Note:**
  
The input is not evaluated for an interconnection to '0'. The input side circuit breaker
is then closed without premagnetizing the transformer.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| 00 | Converter cooling on | OFF | ON | 9795 |
| 08 | Circuit breaker close | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Switch on input transformer premagnetization | OFF | ON | - |
| 18 | Enable door release | No | Yes | - |
| 19 | Switch on line monitoring | OFF | ON | - |
| 20 | Enable automatic offset calculation | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | Switch on additional auxiliaries | No | Yes | - |
| 22 | Activate precharging | OFF | ON | 9833 |
| 23 | Generator excitation enable | No | Yes | - |
| 24 | Enable DC link monitoring | No | Yes | - |
| 30 | Switch on braking chopper | OFF | ON | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6650

### p6230[0...1] Breaker configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 1   [ 1 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for the breaker.  
For p6230[x] = 1, the following applies:  
The simulation mode is automatically deactivated (p6670[y] = 0 signal).  
For p6230[x] = 0, the following applies:  
The simulation mode is automatically activated (p6670[y] = 1 signal).  
The disconnector cannot be selected at the infeed.

**Value:**
  
0:
Breaker not present  
1:
Breaker present

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Circuit breaker  
[
1]:
Disconnector

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6670

### p6243[0...n] PolID motion-based alignment duration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [s] | 15 [s] | [ 0 ] 1 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the wait time when aligning the rotor during motion-based rotor position identification.  
After the motor excitation has built up and the Id current has been specified, the
ramp-function generator enable is delayed by this time.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0346, p1402, p1612

### p6288[0...n] Generator excitation inhibit delay time

### p6289[0...n] BI: Generator test operation signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**CDS, p0170 | **Func. diagram:** 8636 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the generator test operation.  
BI: p6289 = 1 signal  
- test operation is switched on.  
- p6650 is automatically set to 34 (test operation, generator excitation).  
BI: p6289 = 0 signal  
- test operation is switched-off.  
- p6650 is automatically set to 0.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6650

### p6296[0...2] Cooling unit water flow thresholds

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9798 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), VECTOR3P (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), VECTORM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), B_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), BMM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -75.00 [ltr/min] | 375.00 [ltr/min] | [ 0 ] -1.00 [ltr/min]  [ 1 ] 160.00 [ltr/min]  [ 2 ] 5.00 [ltr/min] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the thresholds for monitoring the water flow.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Water flow plausibility threshold  
[
1]:
Water flow alarm threshold  
[
2]:
Water flow hysteresis

**Note:**
  
The following generally applies:  
The factory setting value is appropriately set depending on the equipment description
data.  
For index [0]:  
If the flux monitoring is active (fault threshold (device description data) ! =0),
the following applies to the plausibility threshold setting:  
Wire breakage threshold (equipment description data) &lt; plausibility threshold &lt; -1
liter.  
If the plausibility threshold is set lower than the wire breakage threshold, then
the value is limited to the wire breakage threshold.  
For index [1]:  
The following applies to the alarm threshold setting:  
Hysteresis &lt; alarm threshold &gt; fault threshold (equipment description data) + hysteresis  
If the alarm threshold is set lower than the fault threshold + hysteresis, then the
value is limited to the fault threshold + hysteresis.  
For index [2]:  
The following applies to the hysteresis setting:  
0 &lt; hysteresis &lt; 20 liters

### p6298[0...1] Cooling unit differential pressure min value alarm thresh

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( ) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9799 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), VECTOR3P (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), VECTORM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), B_INFMV (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), A_INFM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit), BMM2C (Cool_sys_mon, Cool_unit) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.1 [bar] | 10.0 [bar] | [ 0 ] 2.0 [bar]  [ 1 ] 0.3 [bar] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the alarm threshold/hysteresis for the minimum differential pressure.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Liquid differential pressure alarm threshold  
[
1]:
Liquid differential pressure hysteresis

**Note:**
  
The following applies to the alarm threshold setting:  
Alarm threshold &gt; fault threshold (hardware description data)  
If the alarm threshold is set lower than the fault threshold, then the value is limited
to the fault threshold (from the hardware description data) + hysteresis (p6298.1).  
The following generally applies:  
The factory setting value is appropriately set depending on the hardware description
data.  
The following applies to the hysteresis setting:  
The hysteresis must be greater than 0 and less than or equal to 6 bar.

### p6299[0...1] Power unit cooling input temperature alarm threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( ) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9797 |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [°C] | 100.0 [°C] | [ 0 ] 40.0 [°C]  [ 1 ] 1.0 [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the alarm threshold for the maximum intake temperature for liquid cooling or
intake temperature for air cooling.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Temperature alarm threshold  
[
1]:
Temperature hysteresis

**Note:**
  
The following generally applies:  
The factory setting value is appropriately set depending on the equipment description
data.  
For index [0]:  
The following applies to the alarm threshold setting:  
20 °C &lt; alarm threshold &lt; fault threshold (equipment description data) - hysteresis.  
If the alarm threshold is set higher than the fault threshold - hysteresis, then the
value is limited to the fault threshold - hysteresis.  
For index [1]:  
The following applies to the hysteresis setting:  
0 °C &lt; hysteresis &lt; 5 °C

### p6420 Phase shift input voltage VSM to the drive converter

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**All groups | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -180.000 [°] | 179.900 [°] | [ 0 ] 0.000 [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the phase shift between the synchronizing voltage measured by the Voltage Sensing
Module (VSM) and the actual drive converter input voltage.

**Warning:**
  
Switching-in with a significantly incorrectly parameterized offset angle (&gt; 5 °) can
cause a peak current intervention and / or triggering the crowbar thyristor.

**Caution:**
  
If this parameter is changed in the "ready for operation" state and if a synchronizing
voltage is already available at the VSM, under certain circumstances, a line supply
fault can be signaled. When this occurs for the first time after changing the parameter,
the fault can be ignored and acknowledged.

**Note:**
  
This phase shift must be determined when commissioning the system.  
Example:  
If the converter input voltage (= secondary side voltage of the power transformer)
lags the synchronizing voltage measured by the VSM by 30 °, then p6420 should be set
to -30°.

### p6422 Line supply voltage rotating field direction

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Setting to reverse the rotating field direction of the synchronizing voltage system
measured by the Voltage Sensing Module (VSM).

**Value:**
  
0:
Rotating field direction positive  
1:
Rotating field negative

**Warning:**
  
Only use in an emergency if it is not possible to correct the wiring. Extreme caution
must be applied in this case when measuring the phase shift (p6420).

**Note:**
  
Allows the rotating field direction to be adapted if there is inconsistency in the
wiring.

### r6440 Infeed identification phase shift U_input VSM/converter

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°] | - [°] | [ ] - [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays the identified offset angle between the input voltage of the VSM and the
connection voltage of the power unit.  
For a positive phase sequence (clockwise rotating field, frequency positive) the following
applies:  
- subtract the result from p6420.  
For a negative phase sequence (clockwise rotating field, frequency negative), the
following applies:  
- add the result to p6420.  
The result should be entered into p6420.  
Example:  
r6440 = 30  
p6420 = 0  
--&gt; p6420 = 0 - 30 = -30

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0066, p3410, p6420

### r6441 Infeed identification line voltage sensing gain adaptation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [%] | - [%] | [ ] - [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays the identified voltage gain of the line supply voltage sensing.

p6421

**Note:**
  
The result should be entered in parameter p6421.

### r6442 CO: Infeed identification method display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 8 | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays the currently executed identification mode of the infeed.

**Value:**
  
0:
Identification off  
1:
Identification rotating field direction  
2:
Identification transformer uk  
3:
Identification Tc Dc  
4:
Reactive power steps  
5:
DC link voltage steps  
6:
Vdc symmetry steps  
7:
Setting precontrol  
8:
Identification transformer offset angle

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3410  
Refer to:
A06400

### p6501[0...62] External digital message, component assignment

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8030 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 32767 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the component assignment of the external digital messages.  
The messages are issued on a component for component basis.  
p6501 = 0:  
The message is always issued.  
p6501 = A, A &gt; 0:  
The message belongs to component A and is issued when component A is active.  
More information is provided in p7100 ... p7108.

0]:
A49000 (F) - BO: r6509.0

### p6507[0...7] External analog message component assignment

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8031 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Messages | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 32767 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the component assignment of the external analog messages.  
The messages are issued on a component for component basis.  
p6507 = 0:  
The message is always issued.  
p6507 = A, A &gt; 0:  
The message belongs to component A and is issued when component A is active.  
More information is provided in p7100 ... p7108.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
A49090 (F) - BO: r6506.0  
[
1]:
A49091 (F) - BO: r6506.1  
[
2]:
A49092 (F) - BO: r6506.2  
[
3]:
F49093 (A) - BO: r6506.3  
[
4]:
F49094 (A) - BO: r6506.4  
[
5]:
F49095 (A) - BO: r6506.5  
[
6]:
F49096 (A) - BO: r6506.6  
[
7]:
F49097 (A) - BO: r6506.7

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6503, p6504, p6505, r6506, p6508

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Cabinet 1 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 01 | Cabinet 1 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 02 | Cabinet 2 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 03 | Cabinet 2 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 04 | Cabinet 3 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 05 | Cabinet 3 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 06 | Cabinet 4 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 07 | Cabinet 4 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 08 | Cabinet 5 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 09 | Cabinet 5 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 10 | Cabinet 6 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 11 | Cabinet 6 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 12 | Cabinet 7 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 13 | Cabinet 7 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 14 | Cabinet 8 fan 1 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |
| 15 | Cabinet 8 fan 2 defective | No | Yes | 9839 |

p6515  
Refer to:
A49143, F49144

## SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLM2C 06522 - 06751

SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLM2C 06522 - 06751

### p6522[0...n] Line transformer rated primary current

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [Arms] | 5000.000 [Arms] | [ 0 ] 420.000 [Arms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the primary rated current of the line transformer.

**Note:**
  
For a three-winding or four-winding transformer (1 primary winding system and 2 or
3 secondary winding systems), normally, the resulting total current is specified while
the relative short-circuit voltage refers to an individual system. In this case, the
primary-side rated current, specified in the data sheet, should be halved or divided
by three.

### p6531 Precharging two-level control enable

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9833, 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the enable signal of the two-level control of the precharging.

**Value:**
  
0:
Not enabled  
1:
Enabled

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6621, r6622, p6623

**Note:**
  
The maximum activation time of the two-level control is defined in the hardware description
data.  
When the two-level control is enabled (p6531 = 1) the following applies:  
The DC link voltage is controlled between the upper and lower thresholds (p6622[0,
1]).  
The DC link voltage actual value is set using p6623[0, 1].

### p6570[0...7] BI: Precharging control signals signal sources

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9833, 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the control signals of the precharging.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Precharging on command from the sequence control  
[
1]:
Precharging circuit is switched on  
[
2]:
Precharging transformer temperature monitor OK  
[
3]:
Precharging off command external  
[
4]:
Precharging on command external  
[
5]:
Precharging on command from power semiconductor test  
[
6]:
Precharging ON command for DSM update  
[
7]:
Precharging fault, precharging converter

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6221

**Note:**
  
For index [2]:  
If there is no temperature monitor, then interconnect to a fixed value.  
For index [3]:  
When the binector input is interconnected, precharging is switched-on independent
of the magnitude of the precharging threshold.  
For index [5]:  
Keep the factory setting.  
For index [7]:  
Keep the factory setting.

### r6571.0...8 CO/BO: Precharging control and status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Activate precharging | OFF | ON | - |
| 01 | Precharging active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Precharging complete | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Switch-on precharging for DSM update | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Switch-on precharging power semiconductor test | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Switch off precharging external | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Precharging switched on feedback | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Temperature monitor precharging transformer responded | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Precharging fault, precharging converter | No | Yes | - |

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

Not active  
10:
Fault  
20:

50:
Active

Switching-off operation  
85:
Recovery process

### r6575.0...29 CO/BO: PU control word/status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9982, 9983 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the control word/status word of the power unit.  
For bit 23 = 1:  
The valve inhibit is active on at least one of the Power Stack Adapters (PSA) assigned
to a drive object (DO).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Command door release on (PSA, DO 3) | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Circuit breaker enable (PSA, DO 1) | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Command precharging on (PSA, DO 2) | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Command circuit breaker undervoltage coil (PSA, DO 0) | Yes | No | - |
| 04 | Status precharging completed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Cell overvoltage fault active | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Status door opened (PSA, DI 1) | No | Yes | - |
| 17 | Status PSA DI 3 | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | Status leakage water detected (PSA, DI 2) | Yes | No | - |
| 23 | Status converter inhibited | No | Yes | - |
| 24 | Status converter in a no-voltage condition | No | Yes | - |
| 26 | Status AVT offset calibration active | No | Yes | - |
| 29 | Status Vdc link monitoring active | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6901, p6902, p6903

### r6580[0...n] CO: PSA digital inputs/outputs status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9982, 9983, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the status of the digital inputs/outputs of the Power
Stack Adapter (PSA).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | DI 0 (X322.8) gate supply voltage (GSV) faulted | Yes | No | - |
| 01 | DI 1 (X322.5) cabinet door opened | Yes | No | - |
| 02 | DI 2 (X322.4) leakage water detected | Yes | No | - |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) Active Line Module (ALM) alarm | Yes | No | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) Active Line Module (ALM) fault | Yes | No | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) reserved | Low | High | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) reserved | Low | High | - |
| 08 | DO 0 (X331.15) command circuit breaker undervoltage coil | Yes | No | - |
| 09 | DO 1 (X331.14) circuit breaker enable | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | DO 2 (X331.11) command precharging on | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | DO 3 (X331.10) command door release on | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | DO 4 (X331.7) command fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | DO 5 (X331.6) command fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | DO 6 (X331.3) command fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | DO 7 (X331.2) command fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) protective breaker GSV tripped | Yes | No | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped | Yes | No | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) protective breaker PSA tripped | Yes | No | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) status fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) status fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) status fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) status fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) protective breaker anticondens. heating tripped | Yes | No | - |

**Note:**
  
DI: Digital Input  
DO: Digital Output  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output

### r6595[0...n] Cabinet 1 auxiliary fan 1 operating hours

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORM2C (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORDM (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFMV (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFM2C (Aux fan ctrl), BMM2C (Aux fan ctrl) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [h] | - [h] | [ ] - [h] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Display of the operating hours of fan 1 in cabinet 1.  
Prerequisite:  
The operating hours were saved in a non-volatile fashion on the Power Stack Adapters
(PSA).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p17750, p17751, p17752

**Note:**
  
This parameter is only relevant for firmware versions lower than V4.8.  
When upgrading from versions lower than V4.8, then the displayed value can be entered
in p17750, p17750 and p17752.  
The operating hours currently used in the software are saved in a non-volatile fashion
on the NVRAM of the Control Unit. They can be read and changed using parameters p17750,
p17751 and p17752.

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [h] | - [h] | [ ] - [h] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Display of the operating hours of fan 2 in cabinet 2.  
Prerequisite:  
The operating hours were saved in a non-volatile fashion on the Power Stack Adapters
(PSA).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p17750, p17751, p17752

**Note:**
  
This parameter is only relevant for firmware versions lower than V4.8.  
When upgrading from versions lower than V4.8, then the displayed value can be entered
in p17750, p17750 and p17752.  
The operating hours currently used in the software are saved in a non-volatile fashion
on the NVRAM of the Control Unit. They can be read and changed using parameters p17750,
p17751 and p17752.
