### p6620 DC link precharging test operation setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [%] | 100.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 10.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the setpoint to precharge the DC link for test operation.  
The setpoint for the precharging refers to the setpoint of the DC link voltage (p3510/2).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3510, p6650

**Note:**
  
The pre-charging test mode is set using p6650 = 3.  
The two-level control (p6531 = 1) only functions for a setpoint above 10 %, as, at
this value, the lower threshold is greater than zero (p6622[1] &gt; 0).

### p6621[0...n] DC link precharging setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 9833, 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 70.000 [%] | 150.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 85.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the setpoint for the DC link precharging.  
The precharging setpoint refers to the rated DC link voltage

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p3510, p6626

**Note:**
  
In the semiconductor test mode (p6650 = 4), the setpoint for precharging refers to
p6626.

### r6622[0...1] CO: DC link precharging setpoint display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9833, 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [%] | - [%] | [ ] - [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the setpoint to precharge the DC link.  
The setpoint for the precharging refers to the setpoint of the DC link voltage (p3510/2).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Upper threshold  
[
1]:
Lower threshold

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6621

**Note:**
  
In the semiconductor test mode (p6650 = 4), the setpoint for precharging refers to
p6626.  
The value for the upper threshold is taken from p6621.  
The value for the lower threshold is calculated from hardware description data.

### p6623[0...1] CI: DC link precharging actual value signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9833, 9834 |
| **Object:**A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the DC link voltage actual value of the precharging.  
When precharging is active (r6571.1 = 1), the following applies:  
The actual value of the DC link voltage via signal source with index 0 is evaluated.  
When precharging is not active (r6571.1 = 0), the following applies:  
The actual value of the DC link voltage via signal source with index 1 is evaluated.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Actual value precharging active  
[
1]:
Actual value precharging inactive

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6571

**Note:**
  
A distinction is made between active and inactive precharging if index 1 was connected.
Otherwise, index number 0 is always valid.

### p6624[0...1] Submodule precharging supplementary setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [%]  [ 1 ] 20.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
For index [0]:  
Sets the supplementary setpoint for the total module voltage during step-up operation.  
For index [1]:  
Sets the voltage hysteresis of the DC link voltage during step-up operation.  
Both values are referred to half of the DC link setpoint (p3510).  
The cells are moderately overcharged as a result of the supplementary setpoint. A
transformer can be premagnetized with the additional cell energy.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Submodule precharging supplementary setpoint  
[
1]:
Submodule precharging Vdc hysteresis

**Note:**
  
The parameters are only active for p3400.2 = 1 (DC link capacitor available).

0]:
Enable

### r6630[0...1] DC link overvoltage tripping threshold crowbar thyristor PSA

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the trip threshold (PSA) for the crowbar thyristor due to a DC link overvoltage.  
r6630[0]:  
Displays the relative threshold referred to the maximum DC link voltage after having
taken into account the altitude derating.  
r6630[1]:  
Displays the absolute threshold that was written into the PSA after commissioning
was completed.

**Note:**
  
PSA: Power Stack Adapter

### p6650 Sequence control test mode activation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 80 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate/deactivate test mode for sequence control.  
For test mode [80]: the CU and DSM should be switched off and on again after the update.

**Value:**
  
0:
Test operation inactive  
3:
Test operation precharging  
7:
Test operation auxiliary fan  
10:
Test operation cooling  
12:
Test operation additional auxiliaries  
15:
Test operation line supply monitoring  
20:
Test operation line side switch 1  
31:
Test operation braking chopper  
34:
Test operation generator excitation  
80:
Test operation DSM upgrade

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0840, p6651  
Refer to:
A07318

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the test mode.

**Caution:**
  
Bit r0899.3 (fault present) can be set with delay in the status word due to the message
generation.

**Note:**
  
The test is started via binector input p6651 = 0/1 signal.  
For p6650 = 15:  
Activates the line voltage monitoring when the converter is ready to be switched on
(p3402 = 200).

### p6655 Acknowledgment external pulse time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 5.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 1.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the pulse time for the external acknowledgment.

**Note:**
  
The total duration of the acknowledgment procedure is extended by this time.  
The pulse time has been selected too short if a fault from the external peripherals
must be acknowledged twice.

### r6660.0...5 CO/BO: Switch 0 control signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays (binector outputs) the control signals for switch 0.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | "Close" command | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | "Open" command | Active | Inactive | - |
| 02 | Command "open undervoltage coil" | Active | Inactive | - |
| 03 | Command "open higher-level switch" | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | Software feedback signal closed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Software feedback signal open | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 03:  
This signal is set if, as a result of the command "open switch", no feedback signal
was detected and the crowbar thyristor was fired. It can be used to switch off the
plant/system by means of a higher-level switch.

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays (binector outputs) the control signals for switch 4.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | "Close" command | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | "Open" command | Active | Inactive | - |
| 02 | Command "open undervoltage coil" | Active | Inactive | - |
| 03 | Command "open higher-level switch" | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | Software feedback signal closed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Software feedback signal open | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 03:  
This signal is set if, as a result of the command "open switch", no feedback signal
was detected and the crowbar thyristor was fired. It can be used to switch off the
plant/system by means of a higher-level switch.

### r6665.0...5 CO/BO: Switch 5 control signals

|  |  |  |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the control signals from switch 5.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | "Close" command | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | "Open" command | Active | Inactive | - |
| 02 | Command "open undervoltage coil" | Active | Inactive | - |
| 03 | Command "open higher-level switch" | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | Software feedback signal closed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Software feedback signal open | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 03:  
This signal is set if, as a result of the command "open switch", no feedback signal
was detected and the crowbar thyristor was fired. It can be used to switch off the
plant/system by means of a higher-level switch.

### p6670[0...5] BI: Switch simulation mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source of the simulation mode for the switch.  
When the simulation mode is activated, the feedback signals for the switch are set
the same as the software commands. All monitoring functions are deactivated.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the simulation mode.

**Note:**
  
BI: p6670[x] = 1 signal: Simulation mode activated  
BI: p6670[x] = 0 signal: Simulation mode deactivated

### p6675[0...5] Switch open monitoring time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.030 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.160 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time of the feedback signals when opening the switch.  
The feedback signal within the time in p6675 must indicate that the switch has reliably
opened (i.e. bounce can no longer be detected). The monitoring time in p6675 must
be set greater than the time in p6676 as it additionally takes into account the bounce
time of the feedback signal.  
The following applies: p6675 &gt; p6676

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6674, p6676  
Refer to:
F07311

**Danger:**
  
If this monitoring function responds, then it must be assumed that the switch had
not opened. In this case, the drive system is not disconnected from the line supply.

### p6676[0...5] Switch max. opening time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.030 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.100 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum permissible opening time of the switch.  
The feedback signal must indicate for the first time that the switch has opened within
the time in p6676. The feedback signal must be stable within the time in p6675 (i.e.
bounce can no longer be detected).  
The following applies: p6675 &gt; p6676

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6675  
Refer to:
F07306

**Danger:**
  
The switch used must open within this time in order to protect the drive system.  
Switch with a longer opening time than specified in this parameter cannot be used.

### p6695[0...n] BI: Undervoltage coil trip signal source 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9983 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets signal source 1 to trip the undervoltage coil of the circuit breaker.  
1 signal: command inactive  
0 signal: command active

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6575, p6696, p6697

**Note:**
  
The undervoltage coil of the circuit breaker is tripped via digital output 0 (DO 0,
X331.15) of the corresponding Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
The AND logic operation of the following signals acts on digital output 0 of the PSA:  
- BI: p6695  
- BI: p6696  
- BI: p6697  
The trip is displayed using binector output r6575.3 = 0.

### p6710[0...5] Switch switching alarm threshold lower

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 100000 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower alarm threshold to monitor the number of times that the switch is operated.  
The lower threshold defines the output for the first alarm.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A07322

**Notice:**
  
After this alarm is output for the first time, the parameters must be set corresponding
to the switching being used.

**Note:**
  
For p6710 = 0, the monitoring for the lower alarm threshold is switched out.

### p6711[0...5] Switch switching alarm threshold upper

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 100000 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the upper alarm threshold to monitor the number of times that the switch is operated.  
The upper threshold defines the output for the second alarm.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A07323

**Caution:**
  
After this alarm is output for the first time, the parameters must be set corresponding
to the switching being used.  
When this alarm threshold is exceeded, the stress on the switch must be checked and
if required, the switch should be replaced.

**Note:**
  
For p6711 = 0, the monitoring for the upper alarm threshold is switched out.

### r6713[0...5] Switch switching operations, total

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays the total number of switching operations of the switch.  
The total switching operations are determined as follows:  
r6713 = r6714 + r6715 * p6712  
r6713: Switching operations, total  
r6714: Switching operations in a no-current condition  
r6715: Switching operations under load  
p6712: Weighting factor for switching operations under load

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6710, p6711, p6712, p6714, p6715  
Refer to:
A07322, A07323

### p6714[0...5] Switch switching operations in a no-current condition

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 100000 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays and sets the number of switching operations of the switch under no-current
conditions.  
When setting the parameter, the value is accepted without confirmation.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6713, p6715  
Refer to:
A07322, A07323

**Note:**
  
The total number of switching operations of the switch under no-current conditions
is saved in a non-volatile fashion on all of the Power Stack Adapters (PSA) being
used.  
Inconsistency is not monitored and there is no automatic alignment of the switching
operations for several Power Stack Adapters. The values of the first Power Stack Adapter
are read and evaluated.

### p6738 CI: Motor model slip adaptation signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the motor model slip adaption.  
The slip frequency is multiplied by the content of the signal source. This allows
the temperature drift of the rotor resistance to be compensated.

p6770

100.00).  
Display value = 100.00 applies: The actual value sensing has failed.  
Display value = 9999.99 applies: The actual value sensing is not available.

| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [%] | - [%] | [ ] - [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the value of actual value channel 9 (X79) for the
Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
The value is displayed corresponding to the set display mode (p6770).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6770

**Note:**
  
The actual value is scaled to the maximum mapping range of the shunt (-100.00 ...
100.00).  
Display value = 100.00 applies: The actual value sensing has failed.  
Display value = 9999.99 applies: The actual value sensing is not available.

### p6770 PSA actual value channels display mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the required display mode for actual value channels for the Power Stack Adapter
(PSA).

**Value:**
  
0:
Not calibrated and with offset  
1:
Poss. calibrated and offset-free  
2:
Offset  
3:
AVT mapping range

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6755, r6756, r6757, r6758, r6759, r6760, r6761, r6762, r6763, r6764, r6765

**Note:**
  
For p6770 = 1 the following applies:  
p6902, p6903 and p6904 define whether the offset calculation should be taken into
account.

17:

I_CONTROL_VG gain negative phase-sequence system  
31:
I_CONTROL_V0M gain zero component positive phase-sequence system  
32:
I_CONTROL_IABG_MIN activation threshold current limiting

### r6778[0...n] CO: DSM register LOCAL

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the status of the currently evaluated double submodule
(DSM).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Alarm "bit error" | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Alarm "poor signal quality" | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Fault "signal loss" | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Fault "corrupted code word" | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Fault "illegal code word" | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Info "all submodules signal signal lost" | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6773, p6784

| 02 | Fault "signal loss" | No | Yes | - |

### p6784 DSM index

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 72 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Selects a double submodule (PSM) to display the properties.  
For the setting 0, the DSM data are displayed cyclically alternating (from DSM00 to
DSM71).  
Parameter r6773 indicates to which DSM (0 ... 71) the displayed values refer.  
For the setting &gt; 0, the data of the selected DSM are permanently displayed (independent
of r6773).  
p6784 = 1: displays the data of double submodule 0 (DSM00)  
...  
p6784 = 72: displays the data of double submodule 71 (DSM71)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6774, r6775, r6776, r6777, r6778, r6779

### p6787[0...3] Voltage measurement filter setting

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 7 | [ 0 ] 4 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the required filter coefficients for the voltage measurement.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Stage 1  
[
1]:
Stage 2  
[
2]:
Stage 3  
[
3]:
Stage 4

**Note:**
  
The voltage measured values are filtered using a recursive filter (4th order low-pass
filter).  
The parameter acts differently on the individual stages of the filter.  
p6780[0...3] = 0: Filter stage 0 ... 3 deactivated  
p6780[0...3] = 1 ... 7: Filter stage 0 ... 3 activated, the filter effect increases
from a minimum to a maximum

### p6788[0...3] Current measurement filter setting

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 7 | [ 0 ] 4 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the required filter coefficients for the current measurement.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Stage 1  
[
1]:
Stage 2  
[
2]:
Stage 3  
[
3]:
Stage 4

**Note:**
  
The current measured values are filtered using a recursive filter (4th order low-pass
filter).  
The parameter acts differently on the individual stages of the filter.  
p6780[0...3] = 0: Filter stage 0 ... 3 deactivated  
p6780[0...3] = 1 ... 7: Filter stage 0 ... 3 activated, the filter effect increases
from a minimum to a maximum

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

4.4

### p6790[0...3] Main fan differential pressure fault threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9806 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00000 | 1000.00000 | [ 0 ] 0.20000 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the fault threshold for the differential pressure for the main fans.  
This is an absolute value in unit "pound per square inch" (psi).  
Fault F49124 is output if the differential pressure falls below this threshold.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
System 1 left cabinet  
[
1]:
System 1 right cabinet  
[
2]:
System 2 left cabinet  
[
3]:
System 2 right cabinet

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6791  
Refer to:
F49124

5.2

Sets the differential pressure for the alarm threshold for the main fans.

Alarm A49111 is output if the differential pressure falls below this threshold.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
System 1 left cabinet

1]:
System 1 right cabinet  
[
2]:

[
3]:

### p6794 Main fan monitoring time when switching on

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9805 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [s] | 60.00 [s] | [ 0 ] 10.00 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time for powering up/powering down the fans.  
If not all of the conditions are fulfilled within this monitoring time after powering
up/powering down, then an appropriate signal is output.

### p6795[0...1] Fan temperature threshold values

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9805 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 20.00 [°C] | 100.00 [°C] | [ 0 ] 30.00 [°C]  [ 1 ] 40.00 [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the temperature threshold values for the air cooling.  
p6795[0]: When this threshold is exceeded, the sequence control of the fan transitions
into state "Fan setpoint 2", r6800[0...63]=5.  
p6795[1]: When this threshold is exceeded, the sequence control of the fan transitions
into state "Fan setpoint 3", r6800[0...63]=6.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6800

### p6798[0...16] BI: Fan feedback signals signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9806 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0...14 ] 1   [ 15 ] 0   [ 16 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the feedback signals from the fans.  
The signals are displayed in r6799[0...5].  
For bit 00,03: Feedback signal thermal protection switch T&gt;5°C

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Thermal protection switch heat exchanger system 1  
[
1]:
Protective breaker power unit system 1  
[
2]:
Monitoring cabinet heating heat exchanger system 1  
[
3]:
Thermal protection switch heat exchanger system 2  
[
4]:
Protective breaker power unit system 2  
[
5]:
Monitoring cabinet heating heat exchanger system 2  
[
6...14]:
Reserved  
[
15]:
DC link precharged system 1  
[
16]:
DC link precharged system 2

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6799

### r6799.0...29 CO/BO: Fan status word/control word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9806, 9807 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the feedback signals and control commands of the fan control.  
For bits 00...05:  
Indicates the state of parameter p6798[0...5].  
For bits 06, 11  
At least one heat exchanger fan of the particular system is active (3 =&lt; r6800[x]
=&lt; 8).  
For bits 16...19:  
Alarm A49111 active.  
For bits 20...23:  
Fault F49124 active.  
For bits 26, 27  
At least one main fan of the particular system is active (3 =&lt; r6800[x] =&lt; 8).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Feedback signal thermal protection switch heat exchanger system1 | OFF | ON | 9806 |
| 01 | Feedback signal protective breaker power unit system 1 | OFF | ON | 9806 |
| 02 | Feedback signal cabinet heating heat exchanger system 1 | OFF | ON | 9806 |
| 03 | Feedback signal thermal protection switch heat exchanger system1 | OFF | ON | 9806 |
| 04 | Feedback signal protective breaker power unit system 2 | OFF | ON | 9806 |
| 05 | Feedback signal cabinet heating heat exchanger system 2 | OFF | ON | 9806 |
| 06 | Heat exchanger fan system 1 | Inactive | Active | 9807 |
| 07 | Command close air flap heat exchanger system 1 | No | Yes | 9807 |
| 08 | Command open air flap heat exchanger system 1 | No | Yes | 9807 |
| 09 | Command supply voltage heat exchanger-inverter system 1 | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Reserved | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Heat exchanger fan system 2 | Inactive | Active | 9807 |
| 12 | Command close air flap heat exchanger system 2 | No | Yes | 9807 |
| 13 | Command open air flap heat exchanger system 2 | No | Yes | 9807 |
| 14 | Command supply voltage heat exchanger-inverter system 2 | OFF | ON | - |
| 15 | Reserved | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | Alarm differential pressure system 1 left cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 17 | Alarm differential pressure system 1 right cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 18 | Alarm differential pressure system 2 left cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 19 | Alarm differential pressure system 2 right cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 20 | Fault differential pressure system 1 left cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 21 | Fault differential pressure system 1 right cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 22 | Fault differential pressure system 2 left cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 23 | Fault differential pressure system 2 right cabinet | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 24 | DSM overtemperature system 1 | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 25 | DSM overtemperature system 2 | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 26 | Cabinet fan system 1 | Inactive | Active | 9806 |
| 27 | Cabinet fan system 2 | Inactive | Active | 9806 |
| 28 | Command close air flap heat exchanger system 1 requested | No | Yes | 9806 |
| 29 | Command close air flap heat exchanger system 2 requested | No | Yes | 9806 |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6798

Fan fault

### r6801[0...7] CO: Fan actual values

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9806 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p0514 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the following values for the main fans:  
- Differential pressure actual values in unit "pound per square inch" (psi).  
- Temperature actual values in degrees Celsius.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Differential pressure system 1 left cabinet  
[
1]:
Differential pressure system 1 right cabinet  
[
2]:
Differential pressure system 2 left cabinet  
[
3]:
Differential pressure system 2 right cabinet  
[
4]:
Temperature system 1 left cabinet  
[
5]:
Temperature system 1 right cabinet  
[
6]:
Temperature system 2 left cabinet  
[
7]:
Temperature system 2 right cabinet

**Note:**
  
The actual values are scaled as follows:  
- r6801[0..3] = p6803[1, 3, 5, 7] x p6804[1, 3, 5, 7]  
- r6801[4..7] = p6803[0, 2, 4, 6] x p6804[0, 2 ,4 ,6]

### p6804[0...7] Main fan scaling factor

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( ) UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9806 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -340.28235E36 | 340.28235E36 | [ 0 ] 1.000000 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

for the main fans.  
The actual values are interconnected via connector input p6803[0...7].

[
0]:
Temperature system 1 left cabinet  
[
1]:
Differential pressure system 1 left cabinet  
[
2]:
Temperature system 1 right cabinet  
[
3]:

- r6801[4..7] = p6803[0, 2, 4, 6] x p6804[0, 2 ,4 ,6]  
For example, the conversion factor from unit "inch water column" to "pound per square
inch" (psi) 0.0360912.

### p6805 Fan speed setpoint selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 10 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Setting to select different speeds for the cabinet fan and heat exchanger fan.

**Value:**
  
0:
Setpoint main fan precharging  
1:
Setpoint heat exchanger fan pre-charging  
2:
Setpoint 1 main fan  
3:
Setpoint 1 heat exchanger fan  
4:
Setpoint 2 main fan  
5:
Setpoint 2 heat exchanger fan  
6:
Setpoint 3 main fan  
7:
Setpoint 3 heat exchanger fan  
8:
Setpoint 4 main fan  
9:
Setpoint 4 heat exchanger fan  
10:
Setpoint fan run-on time

### p6806[0...11] Fan speed setpoints

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [%] | 100.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the speed setpoints for the main fans and the heat exchanger fans.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Main fan 1, heat exchanger fan 2  
[
1]:
Main fan 3, heat exchanger fan 4  
[
2]:
Main fan 5, heat exchanger fan 6  
[
3]:
Main fan 7, heat exchanger fan 8  
[
4]:
Main fan 9, heat exchanger fan 10  
[
5]:
Main fan 11, heat exchanger fan 12  
[
6]:
Main fan 15, heat exchanger fan 14  
[
7]:
Main fan 17, heat exchanger fan 16  
[
8]:
Main fan 19, heat exchanger fan 18  
[
9]:
Main fan 21, heat exchanger fan 20  
[
10]:
Main fan 23, heat exchanger fan 22  
[
11]:
Main fan 25, heat exchanger fan 24

**Note:**
  
The speed setpoints for the speed point selected in p6805 are displayed.  
The values are referred to the rated fan speed.

### p6810 CI: DC link voltage setpoint braking operation signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the DC link voltage setpoint in the braking mode.

**Recommend.:**
  
The parameter should be interconnected with r6643[2] of the BMM2C.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6643

### p6814 Braking chopper total module voltage threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7622 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 30.00 [%] | 135.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 120.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the overvoltage threshold at the double submodules.  
The value is referred to the nominal total module voltage (hardware description data).  
If the actual value of the total module voltage r0070[0] exceeds this threshold, then
a negative current setpoint is generated for the braking chopper and is made available
at connector output r6818 for interconnection; for example, to switch on the braking
chopper.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6817, r6818

### p6817 Braking chopper current setpoint scaling

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7622 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 20 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the current setpoint scaling for the braking chopper.  
This value scales the measured overvoltage for a current setpoint for the braking
chopper.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6814, r6818

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

Display and connector output for the component of the particular drive object at the
current setpoint for the braking chopper.

**Recommend.:**

drive objects.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6817

### p6850[0...n] PSA analog output 0 AND logic operation value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9976 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | FFFF FFFF hex | [ 0 ] 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the value to be ANDed with the signal source in connector input p6840 for analog
output 0 (X332.15/X332.16) of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
Signal sources with data type Unsigned16/Unsigned32 are ANDed with this selected value.  
With the factory setting of p6860 and p6870 the result is displayed as follows at
the analog output:  
- Result of the AND logic operation = 1 --&gt; analog output = 10 V  
- Result of the AND logic operation = 0 --&gt; analog output = 5 V

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6840

### r6888[0...3] CO: PSA analog outputs calibration signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9976 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Calibration signals for the analog outputs of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
For the factory settings of p6860 to p6867 and p6870 to p6877, by appropriately interconnecting
connector input CI: p6840 to p6847 with r6888[0...3], the required calibration signals
are effective at the analog output.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Constant value -1.0 --&gt; 0 V  
[
1]:
Constant value 0.0 --&gt; 5 V  
[
2]:
Constant value +1.0 --&gt; 10 V  
[
3]:
Sawtooth with 1 Hz from -1.0 ... +1.0 --&gt; 0 ... 10 V

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6840

**Note:**
  
When setting the scaling factor (p6842) and offset (p6843) to the factory setting,
the following apply:  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[0] --&gt; analog output = 0 V  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[1] --&gt; analog output = 5 V  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[2] --&gt; analog output = 10 V  
CI: p6840 ... p6847 interconnected with r6888[3] --&gt; analog output = sawtooth 0 ...
10 V with 1 Hz

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |

| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**V/f open-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 200.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Setting of the starting value at zero frequency for the setpoint transmitter.  
U/f control (p1300 = 0):  
The value refers to the unit supply voltage (p0210).  
I/f control (p1300 = 18):  
The value refers to the rated PU current (p0207).

## SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLM2C 06891 - 07592

SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLM2C 06891 - 07592

### p6891[0...n] Setpoint transmitter, maximum amplitude

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**V/f open-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 200.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 100.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum possible amplitude of the setpoint transmitter output.  
U/f control (p1300 = 0):  
The voltage amplitude of the setpoint transmitter output is limited to this value.
The value refers to the rated motor voltage (p0304).  
I/f control (p1300 = 18):  
The current amplitude of the setpoint transmitter output is limited to this value.
The value refers to the rated motor current (p0305).

### p6901 DC link voltage actual values, offset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the offset mode for DC link voltage actual value sensing.  
When the mode is enabled (p6901 = 0) for an operating inhibit, the offset is determined.  
For the inhibited mode (p6901 = 1), the offset is determined – but is not taken into
account for the actual value calculation.  
If the mode is saved (p6901 = 2), the offset determined in enabled mode is saved and
is subsequently taken into account in the actual value calculation, regardless of
the operating state (unchanged).

**Recommend.:**
  
If the mode is enabled (p6901 = 0), the offset is automatically determined when the
converter is in the voltage-free state. If it is not possible to detect that there
is no voltage, then the user must manually perform the calibration (p6901 = 0 in the
no-voltage state, and then p6901 = 2 and then subsequently save the parameter).

**Value:**
  
0:
Offset calculation enabled  
1:
Offset calculation inhibited  
2:
Offset calculation saved

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6575, r6755, r6756, r6757, r6758, r6759, r6760, r6761, r6762, r6763, r6764, r6765,
r6766  
Refer to:
F30106

**Note:**
  
Offset mode can only be set for actual value sensing functions that are available
in the hardware.

### p6902 Current actual values, offset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the offset mode for the current actual value sensing.  
If the mode is enabled (p6902 = 0), for an operating inhibit, the offset is determined
and when operation is enabled, the determined offset is taken into account for the
actual value calculation.  
For the inhibited mode (p6902 = 1), the offset is determined – but is not taken into
account for the actual value calculation.  
If the mode is saved (p6902 = 2), the offset determined in enabled mode is saved and
is subsequently taken into account in the actual value calculation, regardless of
the operating state (unchanged).

**Recommend.:**
  
When the mode is enabled (p6902 = 0), the offset is automatically determined when
the converter is in a no-current state.  
If it is not possible to detect that there is no current flowing (e.g. a slowly rotating
motor without encoder), then the user must manually perform the calibration (p6902
= 0 in the no-current state, and then p6902 = 2 and then subsequently save the parameter).

**Value:**
  
0:
Offset calculation enabled  
1:
Offset calculation inhibited  
2:
Offset calculation saved

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6575, r6760, r6761, r6762, r6763, r6764, r6765, r6766, r6767, r6768, r6769, p6903,
p6950  
Refer to:
F30106

**Note:**
  
Offset mode can only be set for actual value sensing functions that are available
in the hardware.

### p6903 Voltage actual values, offset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the offset mode for voltage actual value sensing.  
When the mode is enabled (p6903 = 0) the operating inhibit of the offset is determined.  
For the inhibited mode (p6903 = 1), the offset is determined – but is not taken into
account for the actual value calculation.

**Value:**
  
0:
Offset calculation enabled  
1:
Offset calculation inhibited  
2:
Offset calculation saved

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6575, r6760, r6761, r6762, r6763, r6764, r6765, r6766, r6767, r6768, r6769, p6902,
p6950  
Refer to:
F30106

**Note:**
  
Offset mode can only be set for actual value sensing functions that are available
in the hardware.

**Description:**
  
Sets the software-related overcurrent limit as a percentage of the trip threshold
on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).

### r6930 CO: Thermal converter model temperature reserve

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8014 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**21_2 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**PMSM | **Scaling:**p2006 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [K] | - [K] | [ ] - [K] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the lowest temperature reserve of the thermal converter
model.  
The value is referred to the actual shutdown threshold of the power semiconductors
(semiconductor valves that can be switched and diodes).

### p6932[0...n] CI: Thermal converter model temperature input signal source 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the temperature input of the thermal model.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6933, r6934

**Notice:**
  
Possible failure of the temperature sensor can result in the semiconductor temperature
being incorrectly calculated. The following response can be set, depending on the
parameterization of p6932/p6933:  
1) p6932 and p6933 are connected with different temperature signal sources. The maximum
from two sources is processed here.  
2) p6932 and p6933 are connected with the same temperature signal source. In the case
that the source develops a fault, internally, the maximum cooling water temperature
(40°C) is assumed. The converter would possibly shut down earlier than necessary.  
3) Either p6932 or p6933 is permanently interconnected to 0, and the other parameters
are connected with the temperature signal source. In the case of a fault, internally,
a cooling water temperature of 0°C is assumed so that the converter would be switched
off too late. This could damage the converter. In this specific case, it is recommended
that p6932 and p6933 are connected with the same temperature signal source.

**Note:**
  
The value is displayed via connector output r6934.

3) Either p6932 or p6933 is permanently interconnected to 0, and the other parameters
are connected with the temperature signal source. In the case of a fault, internally,
a cooling water temperature of 0°C is assumed so that the converter would be switched

**Note:**

| 20 | PSA AT 2 configuration | Fixed | Freely used | - |

### p6944[0...n] PSA analog inputs type

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9984 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0011 0011 0011 0011 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the type of analog inputs on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
In addition, the associated switches S5 and S6 must be appropriately set.  
AI 0: S5.0 = V --&gt; voltage input, S5.0 = I --&gt; current input  
AI 1: S5.1 = V --&gt; voltage input, S5.1 = I --&gt; current input  
AI 2: S6.0 = V --&gt; voltage input, S6.0 = I --&gt; current input  
AI 3: S6.1 = V --&gt; voltage input, S6.1 = I --&gt; current input  
The scaling (final value) can be set using parameters p17460, p17463, p17466, p17469.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | PSA AI 0 , type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 01 | PSA AI 0, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 02 | PSA AI 0, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | PSA AI 1, type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 05 | PSA AI 1, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 06 | PSA AI 1, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | PSA AI 2, type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 09 | PSA AI 2, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 10 | PSA AI 2, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | PSA AI 3, type | Unipolar 10V | Unipolar 20mA | - |
| 13 | PSA AI 3, zero point offset | No | 2V / 4mA | - |
| 14 | PSA AI 3, check plausibility | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6946, r6947, r6948, r6949, p17460, r17462, p17463, r17465, p17466, r17468, p17469,
r17471  
Refer to:
A30125

**Notice:**
  
For operation as a voltage input/current input, switch S5.0, S5.1, S6.0 or S6.1 must
be appropriately set.

**Note:**
  
The settings of the individual inputs can only be changed if these are not preset
by the hardware description (DESC0000).

### p6950[0...2] Actual value monitoring threshold value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 110.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 5.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the threshold value for the actual value monitoring function.  
The value refers to the maximum resolution range of the particular actual value channel.  
For p6770 = 3, this value is displayed for the actual value channel involved in the
corresponding parameter (r6760 and following).  
The total threshold value is displayed there for p6770 = 4.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
DC link voltage  
[
1]:
Output current  
[
2]:
Output voltage

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F30106

### r6951[0...n] CO: PSA temperature input 0 value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9985 |
| **Object:**VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**21_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2006 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°C] | - [°C] | [ ] - [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output of the smoothed value at temperature input 0 (TEMP 0)
on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6955, p6959  
Refer to:
A30018, A30019

**Note:**
  
The input signal can be smoothed for all PSA using p6959[4].  
For 4-conductor temperature sensors, the following applies  
I_IN 0: X333.13  
TEMP 0-: X333.14  
TEMP 0+: X333.15  
I_OUT 0: X333.16  
For 2-conductor temperature sensors, the following external jumpers are required:  
X333.13 - X333.14  
X333.15 - X333.16  
For a display value = -200 °C, the following applies:  
The input is not enabled via parameter p6945 or via the hardware description data.  
For a display value = -199 °C, the following applies:  
An implausible measured value is available at the input.

### p6955[0...n] PSA temperature input 0 offset

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -100.0 [°C] | 100.0 [°C] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the temperature offset for the PT100 measured value at PSA temperature input
0 (PSA TEMP 0).  
If there is a difference between the temperature displayed in r6951 and the actual
temperature, then this offset can be entered in this parameter. thereby compensating
for the difference.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0037, r6951, p6959

### p6959[0...7] PSA analog inputs smoothing time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9984, 9985 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [ms] | 1000.0 [ms] | [ 0 ] 0.0 [ms] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the smoothing time constant of the 1st order low-pass filter for the analog inputs
on the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
This setting is the same for all PDS.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Analog input 0  
[
1]:
Analog input 1  
[
2]:
Analog input 2  
[
3]:
Analog input 3  
[
4]:
Temperature input 0  
[
5]:
Temperature input 1  
[
6]:
Temperature input 2  
[
7]:
Temperature input 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0037, r6951, r6952, r6954, p6955

**Note:**
  
AI: Analog Input  
TEMP: temperature sensor input PT100

### r6992.0...15 CO/BO: Recorder status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144, 8145 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the recorder.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Internal activation | Not set | Set | - |
| 01 | External activation | Not set | Set | - |
| 02 | Internal trigger | Not set | Set | - |
| 03 | External trigger 1.1 | Not set | Set | - |
| 04 | External trigger 1.2 | Not set | Set | - |
| 05 | External trigger 1.3 | Not set | Set | - |
| 06 | External trigger 1.4 | Not set | Set | - |
| 07 | External trigger 2.1 | Not set | Set | - |
| 08 | External trigger 2.2 | Not set | Set | - |
| 09 | External trigger 2.3 | Not set | Set | - |
| 10 | Hardware trigger | Not set | Set | - |
| 11 | Data buffering running | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Post trigger time running | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Data being stored | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Data buffer full | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Trigger group signal | Not set | Set | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6993, p6994, r6997, p6998, p6999  
Refer to:
A49998

### p6993[0...5] Recorder trigger 2 bit mask

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | FFFF FFFF hex | [ 0 ] 0001 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the bit mask for trigger signal 2 (p6994) of the recorder.  
External trigger 2.1 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[0] and the bit mask in p6993[0].  
External trigger 2.2 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[1] and the bit mask in p6993[1].  
External trigger 2.3 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[2] and the bit mask in p6993[2].  
External trigger 2.4 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[3] and the bit mask in p6993[3].  
External trigger 2.5 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[4] and the bit mask in p6993[4].  
External trigger 2.6 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[5] and the bit mask in p6993[5].

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External trigger 2.1  
[
1]:
External trigger 2.2  
[
2]:
External trigger 2.3  
[
3]:
External trigger 2.4  
[
4]:
External trigger 2.5  
[
5]:
External trigger 2.6

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6994

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.

### p6994[0...5] CI: Recorder trigger 2 signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Integer32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for trigger 2 of the recorder.  
External trigger 2.1 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[0] and the bit mask in p6993[0].  
External trigger 2.2 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[1] and the bit mask in p6993[1].  
External trigger 2.3 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[2] and the bit mask in p6993[2].  
External trigger 2.4 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[3] and the bit mask in p6993[3].  
External trigger 2.5 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[4] and the bit mask in p6993[4].  
External trigger 2.6 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[5] and the bit mask in p6993[5].

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External trigger 2.1  
[
1]:
External trigger 2.2  
[
2]:
External trigger 2.3  
[
3]:
External trigger 2.4  
[
4]:
External trigger 2.5  
[
5]:
External trigger 2.6

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6993

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.
