### p6998[0...4] BI: Recorder trigger 1 signal sources

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources to activate and trigger the recorder.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External activation  
[
1]:
External trigger 1.1  
[
2]:
External trigger 1.2  
[
3]:
External trigger 1.3  
[
4]:
External trigger 1.4

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.  
For index [0]:  
The external recorder activation extends the internal activation. If the recorder
has already been activated internally, then it can be externally triggered via p6998[1..4]
or p6994[0..2], without requiring that p6998[0] = 1.

### p6999[0...8] Recorder parameterization

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144, 8145 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2000 | [ 0 ] 1   [ 1 ] 2000   [ 2 ] 1900   [ 3 ] 0   [ 4 ] 0   [ 5 ] 100   [ 6 ] 1   [ 7 ] 1   [ 8 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Setting to parameterize the recorder.  
The recorder supplies up to 256 internal variables (depending on the parameterization).
The maximum recording time is 2000 ms. The variables are acquired in an integer multiple
of the current controller sampling time – and a pretrigger can be set. The values
can then be acyclically written to the memory card.  
The development and system test departments have the software necessary to decode
the content.  
Normally, this recorder is always enabled. However, if problems occur for topologies
with a low remaining CPU time, then it can be deactivated using this parameter (this
saves approx. 5 ... 20 µs CPU time).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Enable  
[
1]:
Recording time  
[
2]:
Pretrigger time internal trigger  
[
3]:
Output message  
[
4]:
Recording factor  
[
5]:
Pretrigger time external trigger  
[
6]:
Hardware trace in synchronism  
[
7]:
Hardware trigger active  
[
8]:
Recording sampling time factor

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49998

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.  
For index [0]:  
Enables or disables the function.  
p6999[0] = 0  
Inhibits the function.  
p6999[0] = 1  
Enables the function. A POWER ON is required after the enable.  
p6999[0] = 20  
Sets the factory setting for p6999, p6996, p6993, p6994, p6998.  
For index [1]:  
Sets the recording time, [ms]. The value is discretized with the current controller
sampling time.  
For index [2]:  
Sets the pretrigger time [ms] of the internal trigger. This time is included in the
recording time and cannot be longer than the recording time p6999[1]. The value is
discretized with the current controller sampling time.  
For index [3]:  
Enables or inhibits the output of message A49998 when a trigger event is initiated.  
For index [4]:  
p6999[4] = n, n = 0 ... 4  
Recording with the factor, which extends the record time p6999[1] and the pre-trigger
time p6999[2] 2^n times, and reduces the number of signals 2^n times.  
Example:  
p6999[1] = 2000, p6999[2] = 1000, p6999[4] = 4  
--&gt; recording time: 2000 ms * 2^4 = 32 s, pre-trigger time:1000 ms * 2^4 = 16 s, number
of signals 128/16 = 8  
When setting p6999[4] to 0, the results are as follows:  
--&gt; recording time: 2000 ms, pre-trigger time: 1000 ms, number of signals: 128  
The maximum number of signals depends on the drive object.  
For index [5]:  
Sets the pretrigger time [ms] of the external trigger. This time is included in the
recording time and cannot be longer than the recording time p6999[1]. The value is
discretized with the current controller sampling time.  
For index [6]:  
Activating/deactivating wait time for a hardware trace to be completely uploaded.  
p6999[6] = 1  
After a trigger event, the system waits for the hardware trace upload to be completed.
A new trigger is not possible until this upload has been completed.  
p6999[6] = 0  
After a trigger event, the system does NOT wait for the hardware trace upload to be
completed. Recorder trace can be immediately re-triggered.  
For index [7]:  
Deactivates/activates the hardware trigger. When p6999[7] = 0, the hardware trigger
is deactivated, and a hardware fault means that the recorder is no longer triggered.  
For index [8]:  
Sets the factor for the recording sampling time.  
Example:  
p6999[8] = 2 --&gt; sampling time to record/trace signals is 2 x current controller sampling
time  
After a power on, this parameter has the same value as before the change.

### r7100[0...99] Par_circuit ring buffer fault/alarm code

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Ring buffer for faults and alarms that have occurred from power units connected in
parallel (Motor Module, Active Line Infeed, Voltage Sensing Module).  
Displays the fault/alarm code.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7101, r7102, r7103, r7104, r7105

**Note:**
  
The last fault case that occurred is documented in index 0.  
The parameter is reset to 0 at POWER ON.

Displays the fault response / alarm response.  
Ring buffer for faults and alarms that have occurred from power units connected in
parallel (Motor Module, Active Line Infeed, Voltage Sensing Module).

**Value:**
  
-1:
NO ALARM  
0:
ALARM  
1:
OFF1  
2:
OFF2  
4:
OFF3  
16:
DCBRAKE  
32:
STOP1  
64:
STOP2  
256:
ENCODER  
1024:
NONE

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7101, r7102, r7103, r7104

The last fault case that occurred is documented in index 0.  
The parameter is reset to 0 at POWER ON.

is displayed if the actual value channel is not enabled.

### p7510 Power unit identification type

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 36 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the identification type for the power unit.

**Value:**
  
0:
Identification off  
10:
Identification C submodule and L arm reactor  
11:
Identification L DC link  
12:
Step response VCQM control loop  
13:
Step response VAGDC control loop DC  
14:
Step response V0G control loop  
15:
Step response V0M control loop  
16:
Step response modulation depth reserve control loop  
30:
Step response VC0M difference control loop  
31:
Step response VCM difference control loop  
32:
Step response VC0G difference control loop  
33:
Step response VCG difference control loop  
34:
Step response IG difference control loop  
35:
NA reserve 1  
36:
NA reserve 2

### p7528[0...n] DC link current limit positive

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 7603 |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.01 [A] | 100000.00 [A] | [ 0 ] 100000.00 [A] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the positive limit for the DC link current.

**Note:**
  
For a positive DC link current, a motor application (VECTORM2C) is motoring.  
In this case, a grid application (A_INFM2C) injects power into the grid.  
The parameter is preassigned when commissioning for the first time.

Sets the negative limit for the DC link current.

**Note:**
  
For a positive DC link current, a motor application (VECTORM2C) is motoring.  
In this case, a grid application (A_INFM2C) injects power into the grid.  
The parameter is preassigned when commissioning for the first time.

### p7530[0...n] DC link voltage setpoint

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 50 [%] | 116 [%] | [ 0 ] 100 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the setpoint for the DC link voltage when the infeed is active in operating mode
p1300 = 21.  
The parameter refers to the rated DC link voltage of the converter.

**Dependency:**
  
The total setpoint of the DC link voltage comprises the fixed value (p7530) and the
supplementary value (p7531). The sum has an upper limit of 116 % of the rated DC link
voltage. The sum has a lower limit defined by p7532.  
  
Refer to:
p7531, p7532

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the supplementary setpoint of the DC link voltage when
the infeed is active in operating mode p1300 = 21.  
The parameter refers to the rated DC link voltage of the converter.

### p7532[0...n] Minimum DC link voltage

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 50 [%] | 90 [%] | [ 0 ] 80 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower limit for the DC link voltage.  
A lower limit is applied to the DC link voltage if the rectified value of the line
voltage falls below the value set in p7532. Power can no longer be drawn from the
DC link. When the Vdc_min controller (kinetic buffering) is enabled, the closed-loop
controller starts to support the module voltages.  
The parameter refers to the rated DC link voltage of the converter.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7530, p7531

### p7535[0...n] Vdc_min controller scaling torque ramp

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1 [%] | 100 [%] | [ 0 ] 100 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the scaling of the torque ramp after intervention of the Vdc_min controller (kinetic
buffering).  
After a power failure and intervention of the Vdc_min controller, when the power supply
returns, the torque is ramped up along a 50 ms ramp. The ramp gradient can be scaled
with p7535, and can therefore be reduced. This can be used to avoid resonance points
being excited in the drive train.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7536

### p7541[0...n] Switchover frequency clocked modulation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 5.00 [Hz] | 300.00 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 34.00 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower switchover frequency, where the circulating currents are modulated,
clocked.  
The ripple on the capacitor currents can be optimized using this frequency.

**Dependency:**
  
The upper switchover frequency is specified in p7542. It must be greater than p7541,
so that a hysteresis is obtained.  
  
Refer to:
p7542

### p7542[0...n] Frequency hysteresis between pulsed/optimized modulation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.10 [Hz] | 300.00 [Hz] | [ 0 ] 2.00 [Hz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the frequency hysteresis between pulsed and optimized modulation of the circulating
currents.  
The ripple on the capacitor currents can be optimized using this frequency.

**Dependency:**
  
The lower switchover frequency is specified in p7541.  
The upper limit is obtained from p7541+p7542.  
  
Refer to:
p7541

### p7543[0...n] Additional frequency upper limit in U/f operation

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.10 [%] | 50.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 5.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the upper limit for the additional frequency in Uf operation. The parameter is
referred to p211. The additional frequency is added to the static frequency value
to dampen the system or to be able to flexibly yield at the current limit.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0211, p7544

| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the lower limit for the additional frequency in Uf operation. The parameter is
referred to p211. The additional frequency is added to the static frequency value
to dampen the system or to be able to flexibly yield at the current limit.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0211, p7543

### p7545[0...n] Maximum conductor-ground voltage

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [V] | 100000.00 [V] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum conductor-ground voltage at the converter terminals.  
This value is automatically preset to 71 % of the rated line voltage or the rated
motor voltage using p3900 or p0340 when commissioning has been completed.  
The value can be increased for motors with adequate insulation; as a consequence,
a higher output current is obtained at low frequencies.

### r7555 CO: Cell voltage control precontrol power

## SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLM2C 07595 - 17407

SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLM2C 07595 - 17407

### p7595 BI: U/f control enable

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source to enable the U/f control.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0056

**Note:**
  
It is only possible to enable the U/f control from a minimum speed of approx. 20 Hz.  
The activation can be seen in r0056.6.

### r7760 Write protection/know-how protection status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the status for the write protection and know-how protection.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Write protection active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Know-how protection active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Know-how protection temporarily withdrawn | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Know-how protection cannot be deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Extended copy protection is active | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Basic copy protection is active | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Trace and measuring functions for diagnostic purposes active | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Reserved, Siemens-internal | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7761, p7765

**Note:**
  
KHP: Know-How Protection  
For bit 00:  
Write protection can be activated/deactivated via p7761 on the Control Unit.  
For bit 01:  
The know-how protection can be activated by entering a password (p7766 ... p7768).  
For bit 02:  
If it has already been activated, know-how protection can be temporarily deactivated
by entering the valid password in p7766. In this case, bit 1 = 0 and bit 2 = 1 offset.  
For bit 03:  
Know-how protection cannot be deactivated, as p7766 is not entered in the OEM exception
list (only the factory setting is possible). This bit is only set if know-how protection
is active (bit 1 = 1) and p7766 has not been entered in the OEM exception list.  
For bit 04:  
When know-how protection has been activated, the contents of the memory card (parameter
and DCC data) can be additionally protected against being used with other memory cards/Control
Units. This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and in p7765.0 is set
= 1.  
For bit 05:  
When know-how protection has been activated, the contents of the memory card (parameter
and DCC data) can be additionally protected against being used with other memory cards.
This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and p7765.1 is set = 1 and p7765.0
is set = 0.  
For bit 06:  
When know-how protection is activated, the drive data can be traced using the device
trace function. This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and p7765.2
is set = 1.  
For bit 12:  
Together with p7755, the bit is used to monitor write protection.  
Bit = 1, if p7755 is not equal to 0 and write protection is active (r7760.0 = 1).  
Bit = 0, if write protection was deactivated. p7755 is set to 0, and when write protection
is activated again, bit 12 remains at 0.

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1 | 500 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Sets the number of parameters for the OEM exception list (p7764[0...n]).  
p7764[0...n], with n = p7763 - 1

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7764

**Note:**
  
KHP: Know-How Protection  
Even if know-how protection is set, parameters in this list can be read and written
to.

### p7764[0...n] KHP OEM exception list

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**p7763 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 65535 | [ 0 ] 0 |

4.5

know-how protection.  
p7764[0...n], with n = p7763 - 1

Refer to:
p7763

**Note:**
  
KHP: Know-How Protection  
Even if know-how protection is set, parameters in this list can be read and written
to.

### p7770 NVRAM action

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the action to be executed for NVRAM data.  
At the end of the action the value is automatically set to 0.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Load NVRAM data to parameters  
2:
Load parameters to NVRAM  
3:
Reset

**Notice:**
  
After action p7770 = 1 no more pulses may be enabled.  
After action p7770 = 2, it is essential that parameters are backed up (p0977 = 1)
and that a warm restart is then performed (p0009 = 30, p0976 = 2, 3). This will apply
the values written.

**Note:**
  
For value = 1:  
This action loads the NVRAM data to the parameters.  
For value = 2:  
This action loads the parameters to the NVRAM.  
If value = 3:  
This action sets parameters p7771 ... p7774 to the factory setting.  
It is recommended to avoid placing unnecessary load on the subsequent upload/download
operation.

### p7857 Sub-boot mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode for the sub-boot.

**Value:**
  
0:
Sub-boot manual  
1:
Sub-boot automatic

**Note:**
  
For p7857 = 0 (manual sub-boot) the following applies:  
The parameter should be set to 1 to start the sub-boot.

### r7871[0...15] Configuration changes drive object

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, A_INFM2C, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Displays the configuration changes on the drive object.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Sum of the following indices  
[
1]:
p0010, p0107, p0108  
[
2]:
Drive object name (p0199)  
[
3]:
Structure-relevant parameters (e.g. p0180)  
[
4]:
BICO interconnections  
[
5]:
Activate/deactivate drive object  
[
6]:
Data backup required  
[
7]:
Reserved  
[
8]:
Reference or changeover parameters (e.g. p2000)  
[
9]:
Parameter count through Drive Control Chart (DCC)  
[
10]:
p0107, p0108  
[
11]:
Reserved  
[
12]:
Write protection and know-how protection status  
[
13]:
Reserved  
[
14]:
Reserved  
[
15]:
Reserved

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r7868, r7870

**Note:**
  
For index [0]:  
When changing one of the following indices, then the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [1]:  
Drive object commissioning: When changing p0010, p0107 or p0108, the value in this
index is incremented.  
For index [2]:  
Drive object name. When changing p0199, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [3]:  
Drive object structure. When changing a parameter that is relevant for the structure
(e.g. number of data sets), the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [4]:  
Drive object BICO interconnections. When changing r3977, the value in this index is
incremented.  
For index [5]:  
Drive object activity: When changing p0105, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [6]:  
Drive object, data save.  
0: There are no parameter changes to save.  
1: There are parameter changes to save.  
For index [8]:  
Drive object changeover of units. When changing reference or changeover parameters
(e.g. p2000, p0304), the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [9]:  
Drive object parameter count. When changing the number of parameters by loading Drive
Control Chart (DCC), the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [10]:  
Drive object configuration. When changing either p0107 or p0108, the value in this
index is incremented.  
For index [12]:  
Drive object configuration. When activating/deactivating write protection or know-how
protection, the value in this index is incremented.

Drive object faults. When changing r0944, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [2]:  
Drive object alarms. When changing r2121, the value in this index is incremented.  
For index [3]:  
Drive object safety messages. When changing r9744, the value in this index is incremented.

### p8612[0...1] CAN drive object server SDO

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN (CAN), CU_MV_DP (CAN), VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0581 hex | 8000 067F hex | [ 0 ] 8000 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the identifier (client/server and server/client) of the additional SDO server.  
Using these SDO servers, it is possible to access manufacturer-specific CANopen objects
of the supported drive objects.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
COB-ID drive object from the client to the server  
[
1]:
COB-ID drive object from the server to the client

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r8610

**Note:**
  
SDO: Service Data Object  
Regarding the drive object Control Unit:  
- Corresponds to the CANopen object 1201 hex  
Regarding the drive object with closed-loop control functions:  
- Corresponds to the CANopen object 1202 hex + 1 * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7)

### p8641 CAN Abort Connection Option Code

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 2 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the fault response for a CAN communication error with output of fault F08700.  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 6007 hex.

**Value:**
  
0:
No response  
1:
OFF1  
2:
OFF2

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p2100, p2101  
Refer to:
F08700

**Note:**
  
When the factory setting is changed, the fault response of fault F08700 is written
to p2100 and p2101. In this particular case it is not possible to change the message
type using p2118 and p2119.

### p8700[0...1] CAN Receive PDO 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9204, 9206 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | 8000 06DF hex | [ 0 ] 8000 06DF hex   [ 1 ] 00FE hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the communication parameters for CANopen Receive Process Data Object 1 (RPDO
1).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PDO COB-ID  
[
1]:
PDO transmission type

**Dependency:**
  
A valid COB-ID can only be set for the available (existing) channel.

**Note:**
  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 1400 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
Transmission types 0, 1, FE and FF can be set.  
PDO: Process Data Object

### p8710[0...3] CAN Receive Mapping for RPDO 1

### p8720[0...4] CAN Transmit PDO 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9208, 9210 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | C000 06DF hex | [ 0 ] C000 06DF hex   [ 1 ] 00FE hex   [ 2 ] 0000 hex   [ 3 ] 0000 hex   [ 4 ] 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the communication parameters for CANopen Transmit Process Data Object 1 (TPDO
1).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PDO COB-ID  
[
1]:
PDO transmission type  
[
2]:
Inhibit time (in 100 µs)  
[
3]:
Reserved  
[
4]:
Event timer (in ms)

**Dependency:**
  
A valid COB-ID can only be set for the available (existing) channel.

**Notice:**
  
For inhibit time and event timer, the following apply:  
A value that is not a multiple integer of the CANopen sampling time is rounded-off.

**Note:**
  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 1800 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
Transmission types 0, 1 ... F0, FE and FF can be set.  
p8848: CANopen sampling time  
PDO: Process Data Object

Corresponds to the CANopen object 1806 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
Transmission types 0, 1 ... F0, FE and FF can be set.  
p8848: CANopen sampling time  
PDO: Process Data Object

### p8727[0...4] CAN Transmit PDO 8

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9208 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | C000 06DF hex | [ 0 ] C000 06DF hex   [ 1 ] 00FE hex   [ 2 ] 0000 hex   [ 3 ] 0000 hex   [ 4 ] 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the communication parameters for CANopen Transmit Process Data Object 8 (TPDO
8).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PDO COB-ID  
[
1]:
PDO transmission type  
[
2]:
Inhibit time (in 100 µs)  
[
3]:
Reserved  
[
4]:
Event timer (in ms)

**Dependency:**
  
A valid COB-ID can only be set for the available (existing) channel.

**Notice:**
  
For inhibit time and event timer, the following apply:  
A value that is not a multiple integer of the CANopen sampling time is rounded-off.

**Note:**
  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 1807 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
Transmission types 0, 1 ... F0, FE and FF can be set.  
p8848: CANopen sampling time  
PDO: Process Data Object

### r8745[0...15] CO: CAN free PZD receive objects 16 bit

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**4000H | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output of the free PZD receive objects (16 bit) using SDO transfer.  
An index does not contain a sensible value if the corresponding object is mapped in
one of the 8 possible RPDO (p8710 ... p8717). In this case, the interconnection and
the SDO transfer must be realized via PZD interface 2 (r8850[x]).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PZD object 0  
[
1]:
PZD object 1  
[
2]:
PZD object 2  
[
3]:
PZD object 3  
[
4]:
PZD object 4  
[
5]:
PZD object 5  
[
6]:
PZD object 6  
[
7]:
PZD object 7  
[
8]:
PZD object 8  
[
9]:
PZD object 9  
[
10]:
PZD object 10  
[
11]:
PZD object 11  
[
12]:
PZD object 12  
[
13]:
PZD object 13  
[
14]:
PZD object 14  
[
15]:
PZD object 15

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p8710, p8711, p8712, p8713, p8714, p8715, p8716, p8717, r8750, r8850

**Note:**
  
RPDO: Receive Process Data Object  
SDO: Service Data Object  
Index 0 corresponds to the CANopen object 5800 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 1 corresponds to the CANopen object 5801 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 2 corresponds to the CANopen object 5802 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 3 corresponds to the CANopen object 5803 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).

... 7).

... 7).

... 7).  
Index 7 corresponds to the CANopen object 5807 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 8 corresponds to the CANopen object 5808 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 9 corresponds to the CANopen object 5809 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 10 corresponds to the CANopen object 580A hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number
0 ... 7).  
Index 11 corresponds to the CANopen object 580B hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number
0 ... 7).

0 ... 7).

Index 4 corresponds to the CANopen object 5824 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 5 corresponds to the CANopen object 5825 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 6 corresponds to the CANopen object 5826 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 7 corresponds to the CANopen object 5827 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).

### p8748[0...7] CI: CAN free PZD send objects 32 bit

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Integer32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**4000H | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for free PZD send objects (32 bit) using SDO transfer.  
The value of an index is not active, if the corresponding object is mapped in one
of the 8 possible TPDO (p8730 ... p8737). In this case, the interconnection and the
SDO transfer must be realized via PZD interface 2 (p8861[x]).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PZD object 0  
[
1]:
PZD object 1  
[
2]:
PZD object 2  
[
3]:
PZD object 3  
[
4]:
PZD object 4  
[
5]:
PZD object 5  
[
6]:
PZD object 6  
[
7]:
PZD object 7

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p8730, p8731, p8732, p8733, p8734, p8735, p8736, p8737, r8761, p8861

**Note:**
  
SDO: Service Data Object  
TPDO: Transmit Process Data Object  
Index 0 corresponds to the CANopen object 5830 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 1 corresponds to the CANopen object 5831 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 2 corresponds to the CANopen object 5832 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 3 corresponds to the CANopen object 5833 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 4 corresponds to the CANopen object 5834 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 5 corresponds to the CANopen object 5835 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 6 corresponds to the CANopen object 5836 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 7 corresponds to the CANopen object 5837 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).

### p8837 IF2 STW1.10 = 0 mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, ENC | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2 | [ 0 ] 2 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the processing mode for PROFIdrive STW1.10 "master control by PLC".  
Generally, control world 1 is received with the first receive word (PZD1) (this is
in conformance to the PROFIdrive profile). The behavior of STW1.10 = 0 corresponds
to that of the PROFIdrive profile. For other applications that deviate from this,
the behavior can be adapted using this particular parameter.

**Recommend.:**
  
Do not change the setting p2037 = 0.

**Value:**
  
0:
Freeze setpoints and continue to process sign-of-life  
1:
Freeze setpoints and sign-of-life  
2:
Do not freeze setpoints

**Note:**
  
If the STW1 is not transferred according to the PROFIdrive with PZD1 (with bit 10
"master control by PLC"), then p2037 should be set to 2.

### p8844 IF2 fault delay

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2410 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, ENC | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [s] | 100 [s] | [ 0 ] 0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the delay time to initiate fault F01910 after a setpoint failure.  
The time until the fault is initiated can be used by the application. This means that
it is possible to respond to the failure while the drive is still operational (e.g.
emergency retraction).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r2043  
Refer to:
F01910

### p8870[0...31] SINAMICS Link PZD receive word

|  |  |  |

| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |

4.8

**Description:**
  
Assignment of a PZD to a telegram word from a SINAMICS Link receive telegram.  
For p8839[0] = 2 (COMM BOARD via interface 1), the following applies:  
- PZD p2050[index] is assigned by means of p8870[index], p8872[index].  
For p8839[1] = 2 (COMM BOARD via interface 2), the following applies:  
- using p8870[index], p8872[index], the PZD is assigned r8850[Index].

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2489 |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Setting to invert the individual binector inputs of the binector connector converter.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Status word 1  
[
1]:
Status word 2  
[
2]:
Free status word 3  
[
3]:
Free status word 4  
[
4]:
Free status word 5

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |

| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Bit 0 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 01 | Bit 1 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 02 | Bit 2 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 03 | Bit 3 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 04 | Bit 4 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 05 | Bit 5 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 06 | Bit 6 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 07 | Bit 7 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 08 | Bit 8 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 09 | Bit 9 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 10 | Bit 10 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 11 | Bit 11 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 12 | Bit 12 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 13 | Bit 13 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 14 | Bit 14 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 15 | Bit 15 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |

4.4

**Description:**
  
Binector output for bit-serial interconnection of a PZD word received via interface
2.  
The PZD is selected via p8899[1].

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Bit 0 | OFF | ON | - |
| 01 | Bit 1 | OFF | ON | - |
| 02 | Bit 2 | OFF | ON | - |
| 03 | Bit 3 | OFF | ON | - |
| 04 | Bit 4 | OFF | ON | - |
| 05 | Bit 5 | OFF | ON | - |
| 06 | Bit 6 | OFF | ON | - |
| 07 | Bit 7 | OFF | ON | - |
| 08 | Bit 8 | OFF | ON | - |
| 09 | Bit 9 | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Bit 10 | OFF | ON | - |
| 11 | Bit 11 | OFF | ON | - |
| 12 | Bit 12 | OFF | ON | - |
| 13 | Bit 13 | OFF | ON | - |
| 14 | Bit 14 | OFF | ON | - |
| 15 | Bit 15 | OFF | ON | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p8898, p8899

### p8898[0...1] IF2 invert connector-binector converter binector output

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2485, 2491 |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, BMM2C, ENC | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Setting to invert the individual binector outputs of the connector-binector converter.  
Using p8898[0], the signals of CI: p8899[0] are influenced.  
Using p8898[1], the signals of CI: p8899[1] are influenced.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Bit 0 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 01 | Bit 1 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 02 | Bit 2 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 03 | Bit 3 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 04 | Bit 4 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 05 | Bit 5 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 06 | Bit 6 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 07 | Bit 7 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 08 | Bit 8 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 09 | Bit 9 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 10 | Bit 10 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 11 | Bit 11 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 12 | Bit 12 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 13 | Bit 13 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 14 | Bit 14 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |
| 15 | Bit 15 | Not inverted | Inverted | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r8894, r8895, p8899

### p8899[0...1] CI: IF2 connector-binector converter signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2485, 2491 |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, BMM2C, ENC | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the connector-binector converter.  
A PZD receive word can be selected as signal source. The signals are available to
be serially passed-on (interconnection).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r8850, r8894, r8895, p8898

**Note:**
  
From the signal source set via the connector input, the corresponding lower 16 bits
are converted.  
p8899[0...1] together with r8894.0...15 and r8895.0...15 forms two connector-binector
converters:  
Connector input p8899[0] to binector output in r8894.0...15  
Connector input p8899[1] to binector output in r8895.0...15

### r8960[0...3] PN subslot controller assignment

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 8 | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Displays the controller assignment of a PROFINET subslot on the actual drive object.  
The display is only relevant for Shared Device.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Subslot 2 PROFIsafe  
[
1]:
Subslot 3 PZD telegram  
[
2]:
Subslot 4 PZD supplementary data  
[
3]:
Subslot 5 PZD supplementary data

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r8961, r8962

**Note:**
  
Example:  
If the parameter contains the value 2 in index [1], then this means that subslot 3
is assigned to controller 2.

### r9406[0...19] PS file parameter number parameter not transferred

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the parameters that were not able to be transferred when reading the parameter
back-up files (PS files) from the non-volatile memory (e.g. memory card).  
r9406[0] = 0  
--&gt; All of the parameter values were able to be transferred error-free.  
r9406[0...x] &gt; 0  
--&gt; indicates the parameter number in the following cases:  
- parameter, whose value was not able to be completely accepted.  
- indexed parameter, where at least 1 index was not able to be accepted. The first
index that is not transferred is displayed in r9407.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r9407, r9408

**Note:**
  
All indices from r9406 to r9408 designate the same parameter.  
r9406[x] parameter number, parameter not accepted  
r9407[x] parameter index, parameter not accepted  
r9408[x] fault code, parameter not accepted

### r9407[0...19] PS file parameter index parameter not transferred

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the first index of the parameters that could not be transferred when the
parameter backup files (PS files) were read from the non-volatile memory (e.g. memory
card).  
If, from an indexed parameter, at least one index was not able to be transferred,
then the parameter number is displayed in r9406[n] and the first index that was not
transferred is displayed in r9407[n].  
r9406[0] = 0  
--&gt; All of the parameter values were able to be transferred error-free.  
r9406[n] &gt; 0  
--&gt; Displays r9407[n] the first index of the parameter number r9406[n] that was not
transferred.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r9406, r9408

**Note:**
  
All indices from r9406 to r9408 designate the same parameter.  
r9406[x] parameter number, parameter not accepted  
r9407[x] parameter index, parameter not accepted  
r9408[x] fault code, parameter not accepted

### r9409 Number of parameters to be saved

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the number of modified parameters and those that have still not be saved
for this drive object.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0971, p0977

**Notice:**
  
Inherent to the system, the list of the parameters to be backed up is empty after
the following actions:  
- Download  
- Warm restart  
- Factory setting  
In these cases, a new parameter backup must be initiated, which is then the starting
point for the list of modified parameters.

**Note:**
  
The modified parameters that still need to be saved are internally listed in r9410
... r9419.

### r9491[0...9] BI/CI of BICO interconnections to other drives

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the signal receiver list (Binector Input/Connector Input, BI/CI) for the
first interconnections between this drive and other drives/drive objects.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r9490, r9492, p9493

**Notice:**
  
A drive cannot be deleted if this list is not empty!  
Otherwise, another drive would continue to attempt to read a signal from a drive that
no longer existed.

**Note:**
  
All indices of r9491 to p9493 designate the same interconnection.  
r9491[x] contains the signal receiver and r9492[x] the matching signal source; p9493[x]
can be set to modify the interconnection.

### r17400.0...23 CO/BO: PSA4 subsystem 1 DI/DO status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9982, 9983, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output to interconnect the status of the digital inputs and digital
outputs of the Power Stack Adapter, subsystem 1.  
The following applies for digital inputs:  
- the value corresponds to the corresponding bit in parameter r4022[0] (power unit
data set 0).  
The following applies for digital outputs:  
- the value corresponds to the status of the associated binector input (p4040[0] ...
p4045[0], p4086[0] ... p4093[0], power unit data set 0).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | DI 0 (X322.8) gate supply voltage (GSV) faulted | Yes | No | - |
| 01 | DI 1 (X322.5) cabinet door opened | Yes | No | - |
| 02 | DI 2 (X322.4) leakage water detected | Yes | No | - |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) Active Line Module (ALM) alarm | Yes | No | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) Active Line Module (ALM) fault | Yes | No | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) reserved | Low | High | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) reserved | Low | High | - |
| 08 | DO 0 (X331.15) command circuit breaker undervoltage coil | Yes | No | - |
| 09 | DO 1 (X331.14) circuit breaker enable | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | DO 2 (X331.11) command precharging on | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | DO 3 (X331.10) command door release on | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | DO 4 (X331.7) command fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | DO 5 (X331.6) command fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | DO 6 (X331.3) command fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | DO 7 (X331.2) command fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) protective breaker GSV tripped | Yes | No | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped | Yes | No | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) protective breaker PSA tripped | Yes | No | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) status fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) status fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) status fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) status fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) protective breaker anticondens. heating tripped | Yes | No | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r4022, p4095, p4096

**Note:**
  
DI: Digital Input  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output  
DO: Digital Output  
The significance of the inputs/outputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs/outputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

### r17401.0...23 CO/BO: PSA4 subsystem 1 DI/DO status inverted

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9982, 9983, 9987, 9988 |
| **Object:**A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output to interconnect the inverted status of the digital inputs
and digital outputs of the Power Stack Adapter, subsystem 1.  
The following applies for digital inputs:  
- the value corresponds to the corresponding bit in parameter r4023[0] (power unit
data set 0).  
The following applies for digital outputs:  
- the value corresponds to the inverted status of the associated binector input (p4040[0]
... p4045[0], p4086[0] ... p4093[0], power unit data set 0).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | DI 0 (X322.8) gate supply voltage (GSV) faulted | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | DI 1 (X322.5) cabinet door opened | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | DI 2 (X322.4) leakage water detected | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | DI 3 (X322.1) status precharging on | Yes | No | - |
| 04 | DI 4 (X323.8) alarm | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | DI 5 (X323.5) fault | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | DI 6 (X323.4) | High | Low | - |
| 07 | DI 7 (X323.1) | High | Low | - |
| 08 | DO 0 (X331.15) command circuit breaker undervoltage coil | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | DO 1 (X331.14) circuit breaker enable close | Yes | No | - |
| 10 | DO 2 (X331.11) command precharging on | Yes | No | - |
| 11 | DO 3 (X331.10) (command door release on) | Yes | No | - |
| 12 | DO 4 (X331.7) command fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Yes | No | - |
| 13 | DO 5 (X331.6) command fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Yes | No | - |
| 14 | DO 6 (X331.3) command fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | Yes | No | - |
| 15 | DO 7 (X331.2) command fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | Yes | No | - |
| 16 | DI/DO 0 (X321.13) (protective breaker GSV tripped) | No | Yes | - |
| 17 | DI/DO 1 (X321.12) (protective breaker SITOP 24 V tripped) | No | Yes | - |
| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) (protective breaker PSA tripped) | No | Yes | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) status fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Yes | No | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) status fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | Yes | No | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) command fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | Yes | No | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) command fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | Yes | No | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) (prot. breaker anticondens. heating tripped) | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r4023, p4095, p4096

**Note:**
  
DI: Digital Input  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output  
DO: Digital Output  
The significance of the inputs/outputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs/outputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

| 18 | DI/DO 2 (X321.11) protective breaker PSA tripped | Yes | No | - |
| 19 | DI/DO 3 (X321.10) status fan 1 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 20 | DI/DO 4 (X321.6) status fan 2 on (arm reactor cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 21 | DI/DO 5 (X321.5) status fan 1 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 22 | DI/DO 6 (X321.4) status fan 2 on (submodule cabinet) | No | Yes | - |
| 23 | DI/DO 7 (X321.3) protective breaker anticondens. heating tripped | Yes | No | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r4023, p4095, p4096

**Note:**
  
DI: Digital Input  
DI/DO: Bidirectional Digital Input/Output  
DO: Digital Output  
The significance of the inputs/outputs with the associated descriptive text is defined.  
The inputs/outputs with the text set in brackets or without text can be freely interconnected;
the text in brackets designates the factory setting.

## SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLM2C 17430 - 17756

SINAMICS Parameter ACTIVE INFEED CONTROLM2C 17430 - 17756

### r17430.0...2 BO: DSM upgrade status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Display and binector output for the status word when upgrading the double submodule
(DSM).

**Recommend.:**
  
A POWER ON is recommended once the upgrade has been completed (r17430.2=1).

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Upgrade inactive | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Upgrade running | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Upgrade completed | No | Yes | - |

### r17431 CO: DSM upgrade progress display

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 100 | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Displays the progress when upgrading the firmware of Double Sub Modules (DSM).

**Value:**
  
0:
Not active  
1:
Upgrade started  
2:
The file is opened  
3:
Module in the upgrade status  
4:
Module memory is cleared  
5:
Module upgrade 0 %  
7:
Module upgrade 5 %  
10:
Module upgrade 10 %  
15:
Module upgrade 15 %  
20:
Module upgrade 20 %  
25:
Module upgrade 25 %  
30:
Module upgrade 30 %  
35:
Module upgrade 35 %  
40:
Module upgrade 40 %  
45:
Module upgrade 45 %  
50:
Module upgrade 50 %  
55:
Module upgrade 55 %  
60:
Module upgrade 60 %  
65:
Module upgrade 65 %  
70:
Module upgrade 70 %  
75:
Module upgrade 75 %  
80:
Module upgrade 80 %  
85:
Module upgrade 85 %  
90:
Module upgrade 90 %  
95:
Module upgrade 95 %  
100:
Module upgrade completed

**Dependency:**
  
The upgrade is configured with p6650 = 80, and is started with binector input p6651
= 1 signal.  
  
Refer to:
p6650, p6651

**Warning:**
  
The DC link is charged while upgrading.

**Note:**
  
An upgrade can take up to 12 minutes.

### r17432 DSM target firmware version

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Displays the version of the file for the firmware of the double submodule (DSM) on
the memory card.  
Value = 0 --&gt; no file available  
Value = 04700402 hex --&gt; V04.70.04.02

### r17433[0...1] DSM firmware version actual

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Displays the firmware version of the double submodule (DSM).  
Value = 0 --&gt; no module connected or in operation.  
Value = 04700402 hex --&gt; V04.70.04.02  
In addition to the DSM firmware version, the bypass identification is also displayed
in bit 12 of the "High" word  
Bypass identification  
1 = cell with bypass  
0 = cell without bypass  
The DSM hardware version is indicated in bits 0-3 of the "High" word.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Lowest firmware version  
[
1]:
Highest firmware version

**Note:**
  
For index [0]:  
Lowest version of all modules.  
For index [1]:  
Highest version of all modules.

connector input p2051).

power.  
Background:  
To exchange process quantities between drive objects with various reference variables,
it is necessary to scale to a common reference variable. Generally, the same common
reference value (e.g. p0514[6]) is set on the send and receive sides.

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |

| 0 [ms] | 20000 [ms] | [ 0 ] 500 [ms]  [ 1 ] 30 [ms]  [ 2 ] 250 [ms]  [ 3 ] 30 [ms]  [ 4 ] 250 [ms]  [ 5 ] 30 [ms]  [ 6 ] 3000 [ms] |

[

[
1]:

[
2]:
Channel pulse length nominal  
[
3]:
Channel pulse length tolerance time  
[
4]:
Pause pulse length nominal  
[
5]:
Pause pulse length tolerance time  
[
6]:
Timeout tolerance time

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source to receive pulses from terminal S for SITOP PSE200U.  
The pulses from terminal S can be received via a digital input.  
The status is displayed via r17571.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F49260, F49261, F49262, F49263, F49264

**Note:**
  
SITOP PSE200U selectivity module  
Using this module, the 24 V output voltage generated by the regulated power supply
can be distributed over 4 load circuits. The circuits are monitored and the status
signaled using terminal S.

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output for the status of the evaluation of SITOP PSE200U.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Monitoring enabled | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Channel 1 without fault | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Channel 2 without fault | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Channel 3 without fault | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Channel 4 without fault | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F49261, F49262, F49263, F49264

### p17745[0...n] Auxiliary fan switchover operating hours difference

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** 9838 |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORM2C (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORDM (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFMV (Aux fan ctrl), B_INFMV (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFM2C (Aux fan ctrl), BMM2C (Aux fan ctrl) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [h] | 4294967295 [h] | [ 0 ] 500 [h] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the difference of the operating hours to switchover between the auxiliary fans.  
The auxiliary fans are automatically switched over if the operating hours of both
fans differ by more than the set value.

### p17746 Auxiliary fan maximum operating time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTOR3P (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORM2C (Aux fan ctrl), VECTORDM (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFMV (Aux fan ctrl), B_INFMV (Aux fan ctrl), A_INFM2C (Aux fan ctrl), BMM2C (Aux fan ctrl) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 [h] | 100000 [h] | [ 0 ] 30000 [h] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum operating time of the auxiliary fan.  
An appropriate alarm is output after the maximum operating time has been reached.  
Monitoring is deactivated with p17746 = 0.

Refer to:
A49146

| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C (Fan), A_INFM2C (Fan), BMM2C (Fan) | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Displays information about alarms and faults as well as non-plausibility of temperature
and differential pressure values for the fans.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Alarm temperature left cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Alarm temperature right cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Fault temperature left cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Fault temperature right cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Unplausible temperature left cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Unplausible temperature right cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Alarm differential pressure left cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 07 | Alarm differential pressure left cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Fault differential pressure left cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 09 | Fault differential pressure right cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 10 | Unplausible differential pressure left cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Unplausible differential pressure right cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Differential pressure too low left cabinet | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Differential pressure too low right cabinet | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6798

### r17920[0...n] CO: Temperature actual value submodule A

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Terminals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2006 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°C] | - [°C] | [ ] - [°C] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output of the smoothed temperature actual value of the semiconductor
module in submodule A of the cell.  
The temperature actual value is available multiplexed.  
The associated cell number can be found in r17922.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6955, p6959

**Note:**
  
For a display value = 0 °C, the following applies:  
Cell and measured value are not available.

### p17980[0...n] BI: Circuit breaker test enable signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**PDS, p0120 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORM2C, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20ACTIVE%20INFEED%20CONTROLM2C.md#sinamics-alarms-active-infeed-controlm2c) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the circuit breaker test enable.  
1 signal: test enabled.  
0 signal: test not enabled.

**Note:**
  
The circuit breaker test enable is realized via digital output 1 (DO 1, X331.14) of
the corresponding Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
The test enable is indicated at binector output r6575.1 = 1.
